name: Sandbag - V Sit Twists
instructions: Grab a sandbag of the required weight and take a seat on the floor. Hold the bag above your hips (flexing slightly at the elbow) and create an imaginary 'V' shape with your body by leaning back and lifting your feet off the floor. Tip: Your knees should be bent and if any pressure comes through your lower back, replace your feet back onto the floor. This is the starting position.
Twist your torso to the right hand side until the bag is just off the floor. Exhale as you twist, making sure that your head follows your shoulders.
Reverse the movement towards the left side keeping your breathing constant and exhaling as the weight is lowered either side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: V Sit With Plate Rotations or Seated Barbell Twists.

en: Grab a sandbag of the required weight and take a seat on the floor. Hold the bag above your hips (flexing slightly at the elbow) and create an imaginary 'V' shape with your body by leaning back and lifting your feet off the floor. Tip: Your knees should be bent and if any pressure comes through your lower back, replace your feet back onto the floor. This is the starting position.
Twist your torso to the right hand side until the bag is just off the floor. Exhale as you twist, making sure that your head follows your shoulders.
Reverse the movement towards the left side keeping your breathing constant and exhaling as the weight is lowered either side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: V Sit With Plate Rotations or Seated Barbell Twists.
es: Coge un sandbag del peso requerido y siéntate en el suelo. Sostén el sandbag por encima de tus caderas (flexionando ligeramente el codo) y crea una forma de "V" imaginaria con tu cuerpo inclinándote hacia atrás y levantando los pies del suelo. Consejo: tus rodillas deben estar dobladas y, si recibe alguna presión en la zona lumbar, vuelva a colocar los pies en el suelo. Esta es la posición inicial.
Gira el torso hacia el lado derecho hasta que el sandbag esté apenas separada del suelo. Exhala mientras giras, asegurándote de que tu cabeza sigue a tus hombros.
Invierte el movimiento hacia el lado izquierdo manteniendo la respiración constante y exhalando a medida que se baja el peso a cada lado.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Sentado en V con rotaciones de placa o giros con barra sentado.

name: Sandbag - Leg Raises
instructions: Grab a sandbag and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the sandbag above your head. Elevate your legs (at full extension) vertically towards the ceiling until you create a 90 degree bend at your hips. Tip: Keep your legs extended throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and start to descend your legs on an arcing motion towards the floor. Continue to descend until your legs are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Reverse Crunches.

en: Grab a sandbag and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the sandbag above your head. Elevate your legs (at full extension) vertically towards the ceiling until you create a 90 degree bend at your hips. Tip: Keep your legs extended throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and start to descend your legs on an arcing motion towards the floor. Continue to descend until your legs are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Reverse Crunches.
es: Toma un sandbag y acuéstate boca arriba, tus brazos deben sostener el sandbag sobre tu cabeza. Eleva las piernas (en extensión completa) verticalmente hacia el techo hasta crear una curva de 90 grados en las caderas. Consejo: Mantén las piernas extendidas durante todo el movimiento. Esta es tu posición inicial.
Para iniciar el movimiento, inhala y comienza a descender las piernas formando un arco hacia el suelo. Continúa descendiendo hasta que tus piernas queden paralelas al suelo.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala y regresa las piernas por el mismo camino hasta tu posición inicial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Elevaciones de piernas colgantes o abdominales inversos.

name: Sandbag - Lateral Drag With Press Ups
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and get into a push-up position leaning forward so that your weight is now baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. The sandbag should be placed under your chest to either the right or left side. This will be your starting position.
With your right arm, pull the sandbag from your left-hand side and move it to just outside of your right shoulder.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whilst flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.
Alternate between left and right.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sandbag lateral drags

name.en: Sandbag - Lateral Drag With Press Ups

en: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and get into a push-up position leaning forward so that your weight is now baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. The sandbag should be placed under your chest to either the right or left side. This will be your starting position.
With your right arm, pull the sandbag from your left-hand side and move it to just outside of your right shoulder.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whilst flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.
Alternate between left and right.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sandbag lateral drags
es: Para comenzar este ejercicio, arrodíllate en el suelo y ponte en posición de flexión inclinándote hacia adelante de modo que tu peso quede ahora sobre tus brazos. Coloca tus manos justo afuera de donde estaría tu pecho. Ahora debes ponerte de pie de manera que tu cuerpo quede tuspendido sobre el suelo, los únicos puntos de contacto (con el suelo) deben ser tus manos y pies. Tu cuerpo debe estar en línea recta desde la cabeza hasta los pies y tus brazos perpendiculares al suelo debajo de tu pecho. El sandbag debe colocarse debajo de tu pecho hacia el lado derecho o izquierdo. Esta es tu posición inicial.
Con el brazo derecho, neumático del sandbag desde el lado izquierdo y muévalo justo fuera del hombro derecho.

Mientras inhalas, baje lentamente el pecho hacia el suelo mientras abre los codos. Cuando esté aproximadamente a una pulgada del suelo, debería haber creado un triángulo con las posiciones de tus manos y la línea de tus ojos.

Haga una pausa para un breve movimiento, exhala y presione con las palmas para que tu cuerpo vuelve por el mismo camino a la posición inicial. Consejo: apriete los músculos pectorales mientras lo haces y cuando llegue a la cima del movimiento, no bloquee los codos.
Alterna entre izquierda y derecha.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Arrastre lateral con sandbag

aliases: Sandbag - Lateral Drag With Press Ups
Sandbag Lateral Drag With Press Ups
Lateral Drags With Press Ups - Sandbag
Sandbag - Lateral Drags With Press Up
Sandbag Lateral Drags With Press Up

name: Sandbag - Lateral Drags
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and get into a push-up position leaning forward so that your weight is now baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. The sandbag should be placed under your chest to either the right or left side. This will be your starting position.
With your right arm, pull the sandbag from your left-hand side and move it to just outside of your right shoulder.
Alternate between left and right.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sandbag lateral drag with press ups

en: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and get into a push-up position leaning forward so that your weight is now baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. The sandbag should be placed under your chest to either the right or left side. This will be your starting position.
With your right arm, pull the sandbag from your left-hand side and move it to just outside of your right shoulder.
Alternate between left and right.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sandbag lateral drag with press ups

Coloca tus manos justo afuera de donde estaría tu pecho. Con el brazo derecho, neumático del sandbag desde el lado izquierdo y muévalo justo fuera del hombro derecho.
Alterna entre izquierda y derecha.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Arrastre lateral con sandbag con flexiones

name: Sandbag - Bent Over Rows
instructions: Grab the sandbag (palms facing each other), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. The sandbag should hang directly in front of you as your arms are perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift the sandbag to your abdomen (as you exhale), elevating elbows towards the ceiling (do not exert any force with the forearm other than holding the sandbag). At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the bag to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues. A Low Cable Row is a better choice for people with back pain.
Ensure perfect form and never round the back as this can cause injury.
Be cautious with the weights you are using. If you are in doubt, use a lesser weight.
Variations: Low Cable Row or a Bent Over Barbell Row

en: Grab the sandbag (palms facing each other), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. The sandbag should hang directly in front of you as your arms are perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift the sandbag to your abdomen (as you exhale), elevating elbows towards the ceiling (do not exert any force with the forearm other than holding the sandbag). At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the bag to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues. A Low Cable Row is a better choice for people with back pain.
Ensure perfect form and never round the back as this can cause injury.
Be cautious with the weights you are using. If you are in doubt, use a lesser weight.
Variations: Low Cable Row or a Bent Over Barbell Row
es: Agarra el sandbag (con las palmas una frente a la otra), dobla ligeramente las rodillas y lleve el torso hacia adelante doblando las caderas; Mientras te inclinas, asegúrate de mantener la espalda recta hasta que quede casi paralela al suelo. Consejo: asegúrate de mantener la columna en una posición neutral. El sandbag debe colgar directamente frente a ti mientras tus brazos están perpendiculares al suelo y a tu torso. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Mientras mantiene el torso inmóvil, levanta el sandbag hasta tu abdomen (mientras exhala), elevando los codos hacia el techo (no ejerza ninguna fuerza con el antebrazo que no sea sostener el sandbag). En la posición más contraída, aprieta los músculos de la espalda y mantén la posición durante un segundo.
Baje lentamente el sandbag a la posición inicial mientras inhala.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Precaución:
Este ejercicio no se recomienda para personas con problemas de espalda. Un Low Cable Row es una mejor opción para las personas con dolor de espalda.
Asegúrate de que la forma sea perfecta y nunca redondee la espalda, ya que esto puedes causar lesiones.
Tenga cuidado con las pesas que está utilizando. Si tienes dudas, utiliza un peso menor.
Variaciones: remo bajo con cable o remo inclinado con barra

name: Sandbag - Overhead Extensions
instructions: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, overhand grip. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders, keep your elbows tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the ceiling. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbows pinned in close to your ears. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the bag down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbows are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then exhale and return the bag along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Skullcrushers or Tricep Dips.

name.es: Sandbag - Extensiones por encima de la cabeza

en: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, overhand grip. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders, keep your elbows tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the ceiling. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbows pinned in close to your ears. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the bag down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbows are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then exhale and return the bag along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Skullcrushers or Tricep Dips.
es: Coge un sandbag y adopta un agarre prono a la anchura de los hombros. Levanta el sandbag por encima de tu cabeza para que quede directamente sobre tus hombros, mantén los codos pegados a las orejas y los nudillos mirando hacia el techo. Consejo: mantén la espalda recta durante todo este movimiento y mantén los codos pegados a las orejas. Esta es tu posición inicial.
Mientras la parte superior de los brazos permanece inmóvil, inhala y baje el sandbag con un movimiento de arco detrás de la cabeza hasta que los codos formen un ángulo agudo (menos de 90 grados).
Haga una pausa por un breve momento, luego exhala y vuelve el sandbag por el mismo camino hasta la posición inicial mientras contrae los tríceps.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Skullcrushers o Tricep Dips.

name: Sandbag - Standing Chest Press
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a sandbag at chest height.
2) Push the sandbag straight out in front of you until your arms are fully extended.
3) Pause briefly, then slowly return the sandbag to the starting position.
4) Keep your core engaged and maintain a stable stance throughout the movement.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
This exercise targets your chest muscles and improves upper body strength.

name.es: Sandbag - Press de pecho de pie

en: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a sandbag at chest height.
2) Push the sandbag straight out in front of you until your arms are fully extended.
3) Pause briefly, then slowly return the sandbag to the starting position.
4) Keep your core engaged and maintain a stable stance throughout the movement.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
This exercise targets your chest muscles and improves upper body strength.
es: 1) Párate con los pies separados a la anchura de los hombros, sosteniendo un sandbag a la altura del pecho.
2) Empuja el sandbag hacia afuera frente a ti hasta que tus brazos estén completamente extendidos.
3) Haga una pausa breve y luego vuelve lentamente el sandbag a la posición inicial.
4) Mantén tu core comprometido y mantén una postura estable durante todo el movimiento.
5) Repite hasta el número de repeticiones deseado.
Este ejercicio apunta a los músculos del pecho y mejora la fuerza de la parte superior del cuerpo.

name: Sandbag - Front Raises
instructions: Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Stand upright maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with sandbag just below hips. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating both arms on an arcing motion in front of you. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arms are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arms along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variation: Front Plate Raise or Alternating Front Raises.

en: Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Stand upright maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with sandbag just below hips. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating both arms on an arcing motion in front of you. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arms are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arms along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variation: Front Plate Raise or Alternating Front Raises.
es: Coge un sandbag con un anchura de los hombros por encima de la empuñadura. Colócate de pie erguido manteniendo la espalda recta y permitiendo que los brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo con una sandbag justo debajo de las caderas. En este punto, tu pecho, hombros y cabeza deben mirar hacia adelante. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Inicie el movimiento exhalando y elevando ambos brazos en un movimiento de arco frente a ti. Mantén una ligera flexión en el codo y continúa elevándolo hacia arriba hasta que tus brazos queden paralelos al suelo.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala y regresa los brazos por el mismo camino hasta la posición inicial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones y cambie de brazo.
Variación: Elevación de placa frontal o elevaciones frontales alternas.

name: Sandbag - Bicep Curls
instructions: Grab the sandbag with both hands, in a standing position, with your arms at full length, palms facing in. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract your biceps and bring the sandbag up to shoulder height. Hold the contraction for a second as you squeeze your biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Inhale as you lower the sandbag toward the starting position.
Continue for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rotating Bicep Curls or Standing Barbell Curls.

en: Grab the sandbag with both hands, in a standing position, with your arms at full length, palms facing in. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract your biceps and bring the sandbag up to shoulder height. Hold the contraction for a second as you squeeze your biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Inhale as you lower the sandbag toward the starting position.
Continue for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rotating Bicep Curls or Standing Barbell Curls.
es: Coge el sandbag con ambas manos, en posición de pie, con los brazos extendidos y las palmas hacia adentro. Esta es tu posición inicial.
Mientras exhalass, contrae los bíceps y lleva el sandbag a la anchura de los hombros. Mantén la contracción por un segundo mientras aprietas el bíceps. Consejo: Sólo deben moverse los antebrazos.
Inhala mientras bajas el sandbag hacia la posición inicial.
Continúa con el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Curl de bíceps giratorio sentado o Curl de pie con barra.

name: Sandbag - Upright Row
instructions: Grab a sandbag with a narrow, overhand grip. Adopt a standing position with the bag resting against your thighs, your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the bag as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the sandbag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Upright Rows or Smith Machine Upright Row

en: Grab a sandbag with a narrow, overhand grip. Adopt a standing position with the bag resting against your thighs, your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the bag as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the sandbag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Upright Rows or Smith Machine Upright Row
es: Coge un sandbag con un agarre estrecho y por encima de la cabeza. Adopta una posición de pie con el sandbag apoyada contra los muslos y los brazos colgando perpendiculares al suelo. Esta es tu posición inicial. Consejo: mantén la espalda recta y mantén el sandbag lo más cerca posible de tu cuerpo durante todo el movimiento.

Exhala y neumático del sandbag hacia la barbilla contrayendo los hombros y abriendo bien los codos. Continúa tirando del sandbag hacia arriba hasta llegar a la barbilla. En este punto, tus codos deben estar por encima de el sandbag.
Haga una pausa por un breve momento, luego inhala y vuelve el sandbag por el mismo camino hacia la posición inicial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: remo vertical con mancuernas o remo vertical con máquina Smith

name: Sandbag - Snatch
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the bag with a neutral grip (palms facing together) and stand upright with the bag resting against your thighs. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time lower the sandbag to the floor and rotate your hands so you have an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hands towards the floor to emphasise the power generated by your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag above your head until your arms are nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the bag so that your palms are facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the sandbag along the same path back in front of your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Clean and Press or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

en: Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the bag with a neutral grip (palms facing together) and stand upright with the bag resting against your thighs. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time lower the sandbag to the floor and rotate your hands so you have an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hands towards the floor to emphasise the power generated by your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag above your head until your arms are nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the bag so that your palms are facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the sandbag along the same path back in front of your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Clean and Press or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.
es: Agarra un sandbag con ambas manos y adopta una postura ligeramente más ancha que el ancho de los hombros. Sujeta el sandbag con un agarre neutral (con las palmas juntas) y colócate de pie erguido con el sandbag apoyada contra tus muslos. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, inhala y dobla las rodillas y las caderas para que los muslos queden paralelos al suelo. Al mismo tiempo, baje el sandbag al suelo y gire las manos para poder agarrarlo por encima de la cabeza (con las palmas hacia atrás). Debes sentir como si estuvieras empujando tus manos hacia el suelo para enfatizar la potencia generada por tus piernas.

Inmediatamente después de hacer esto, exhala y usa este poder para atravesar tus talones, muslos y caderas para tirar del sandbag por encima de tu cabeza hasta que tus brazos estén casi bloqueados. Mientras lo haces, voltee el sandbag de modo que sus palmas miren hacia adelante con el peso deslizándose por la parte posterior de tus antebrazos.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala y regresa el sandbag por el mismo camino frente a tus piernas mientras te pones de sentadilla en la siguiente repetición.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variación: Limpieza y press con barra o Limpieza y press con mancuerna de un solo brazo.

name: Sandbag - Sumo Squats
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.
After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.

en: Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.
After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.
es: Coge un sandbag con ambas manos y párate derecho. Mantén esta postura recta durante todo el movimiento y tus brazos permanecen pasivos y extendidos. Adopta una postura amplia con las puntas de los pies ligeramente hacia afuera. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Inhala mientras desciende doblando las rodillas. Recuerde mantener las rodillas detrás de las puntas de los pies y poner la mayor parte de tu peso en los talones. El sandbag ahora debería estar apenas levantado del suelo con los brazos entre las piernas.
Después de una breve pausa, empuja los talones y los muslos para que el torso vuelva a la posición inicial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Precaución: asegúrate de mantener la postura, ya que no hacerlo podría provocar lesiones.
Variaciones: Press de piernas en posición amplia o sentadillas sumo con kettlebells.

name: Sandbag - Sumo Squat To Upright Row
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.

After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up. Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the bag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.

name.en: Sandbag - Sumo Squat To Upright Row
name.es: Sandbag: sentadilla de sumo hasta remo vertical

en: Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.

After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up. Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the bag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.
es: Coge un sandbag con ambas manos y párate derecho. Mantén esta postura recta durante todo el movimiento y tus brazos permanecen pasivos y extendidos. Adopta una postura amplia con las puntas de los pies ligeramente hacia afuera. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Inhala mientras desciende doblando las rodillas. Recuerde mantener las rodillas detrás de las puntas de los pies y poner la mayor parte de tu peso en los talones. El sandbag ahora debería estar apenas levantado del suelo con los brazos entre las piernas.

Después de una breve pausa, empuja los talones y los muslos para volver a levantar el torso. Exhala y neumático del sandbag hacia la barbilla contrayendo los hombros y abriendo bien los codos. Continúa tirando del sandbag hacia arriba hasta llegar a la barbilla. En este punto, tus codos deben estar por encima de el sandbag.
Haga una pausa breve, luego inhala y vuelve el sandbag por el mismo camino hacia la posición inicial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Precaución: asegúrate de mantener la postura, ya que no hacerlo podría provocar lesiones.
Variaciones: Press de piernas en posición amplia o sentadillas sumo con kettlebells.

aliases: Sandbag - Sumo Squat To Upright Row
Sandbag Sumo Squat To Upright Row
Sumo Squat To Upright Row - Sandbag

name: Sandbag - Good Mornings
instructions: Grab a sandbag and place on your upper back taking the weight across your shoulders. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees.
Adopt a shoulder-width stance, maintain a straight back throughout and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to lean forward from your waist. Push your hips and glutes backward as you do so until your torso is just off being parallel to the floor. Your hamstrings should be under tension at this point.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, thrusting your hips forward, squeezing your glutes as you do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed. (Remember to keep your back straight throughout the whole movement).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from any back injuries, this may not be the most suitable exercise for you.
Variations: Barbell Good Mornings or Stiff Leg Dumbbell Deadlifts.

en: Grab a sandbag and place on your upper back taking the weight across your shoulders. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees.
Adopt a shoulder-width stance, maintain a straight back throughout and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to lean forward from your waist. Push your hips and glutes backward as you do so until your torso is just off being parallel to the floor. Your hamstrings should be under tension at this point.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, thrusting your hips forward, squeezing your glutes as you do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed. (Remember to keep your back straight throughout the whole movement).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from any back injuries, this may not be the most suitable exercise for you.
Variations: Barbell Good Mornings or Stiff Leg Dumbbell Deadlifts.
es: Coge un sandbag y colóquelo en la parte superior de la espalda, llevando el peso sobre los hombros. Mantén la espalda recta y una pequeña flexión en las rodillas.
Adopta una postura a la anchura de los hombros, mantén la espalda recta en todo momento y mantén la cabeza erguida. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Inhala y comienza a inclinarte hacia adelante desde tu cintura. Empuja las caderas y los glúteos hacia atrás mientras lo haces hasta que el torso quede paralelo al suelo. Tus isquiosurales deberían estar bajo tensión en este punto.

Después de una breve pausa, exhala y regresa el sandbag a la posición inicial siguiendo el mismo camino, empujando las caderas hacia adelante y apretando los glúteos mientras lo hacess. Colócate de pie firme con las piernas completamente extendidas, la espalda recta y los hombros relajados. (Recuerda mantener la espalda recta durante todo el movimiento).
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Precaución: si sufre alguna lesión en la espalda, es posible que este no sea el ejercicio más adecuado para usted.
Variaciones: Buenos días con barra o peso muerto con mancuernas con piernas rígidas.

name: Sandbag - Alternating Swings
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the sandbag with your palms facing each other and stand upright with the weight in front of you. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the sandbag to your right knee and push your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.
Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag to head height with your arm fully extended as you go.
Inhale and return the sandbag along the same path but on the opposite (left) side as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or TRX Squat Jumps.

en: Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the sandbag with your palms facing each other and stand upright with the weight in front of you. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the sandbag to your right knee and push your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.
Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag to head height with your arm fully extended as you go.
Inhale and return the sandbag along the same path but on the opposite (left) side as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or TRX Squat Jumps.
es: Agarra un sandbag con ambas manos y adopta una postura ligeramente más ancha que el ancho de los hombros. Sujeta el sandbag con las palmas una frente a la otra y colócate de pie erguido con el peso frente a ti. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, inhala y dobla las rodillas y las caderas para que los muslos queden paralelos al suelo. Al mismo tiempo, lleva el sandbag a tu rodilla derecha y empuja tu mano hacia el suelo para enfatizar la potencia generada en tus piernas.

Inmediatamente después de hacer esto, exhala y usa este poder para atravesar los talones, los muslos y las caderas para tirar del sandbag a la altura de la cabeza con el brazo completamente extendido a medida que avanzas.
Inhala y regresa el sandbag por el mismo camino pero en el lado opuesto (izquierdo) mientras te agachas en la siguiente repetición.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones y cambia de lado.
Variaciones: saltos de sentadilla con mancuernas o saltos de sentadilla TRX.

name: Sandbag - Scissor Lunges (Back)
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed on your back. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.

As you exhale, you need to direct force through your quadriceps and glutes in order to drive your body upward and into a jumping motion. As you leave the floor you will reverse your stance by bringing your back leg forward and your front leg back.
As you land, you land into the next repetition by bending at your knees and hips to create right angles at the joints of your front leg, inhale as you do so.
Alternate in this manner and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Barbell Lunges.

en: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed on your back. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.

As you exhale, you need to direct force through your quadriceps and glutes in order to drive your body upward and into a jumping motion. As you leave the floor you will reverse your stance by bringing your back leg forward and your front leg back.
As you land, you land into the next repetition by bending at your knees and hips to create right angles at the joints of your front leg, inhale as you do so.

Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Barbell Lunges.
es: Párate derecho con el sandbag colocado sobre tu espalda. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Da un paso hacia adelante (un poco más grande que un paso normal) y, cuando aterrices, deja caer el cuerpo para crear dos ángulos rectos en las rodillas. Inhala mientras lo hacess manteniendo el torso recto.
Asegúrate de mantener la rodilla delantera sobre el talón y no sobre las puntas de los pies. Tu espinilla delantera ahora debería estar perpendicular al suelo.

Mientras exhalass, debes dirigir la fuerza a través de los cuádriceps y los glúteos para impulsar tu cuerpo hacia arriba y realizar un movimiento de salto. Al levantarte del suelo, invertirás tu postura llevando la pierna trasera hacia adelante y la pierna delantera hacia atrás.
A medida que aterrizas, aterrizas en la siguiente repetición doblando las rodillas y las caderas para crear ángulos rectos en las articulaciones de la pierna delantera, inhala mientras lo hacess.
Alterne de esta manera y repite hasta el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: zancadas caminando con mancuernas o zancadas con barra.

name: Sandbag - Scissor Lunges (Front)
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed on your chest. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.

Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Barbell Lunges.

es: Párate derecho con el sandbag colocado sobre tu pecho. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Da un paso hacia adelante (un poco más grande que un paso normal) y, cuando aterrices, deja caer el cuerpo para crear dos ángulos rectos en las rodillas. Inhala mientras lo hacess manteniendo el torso recto.

A medida que aterrizas, aterrizas en la siguiente repetición doblando las rodillas y las caderas para crear ángulos rectos en las articulaciones de la pierna delantera, inhala mientras lo hacess.

name: Sandbag - Squat Jumps
instructions: Whilst in a standing position, grab the sandbag and place across your back. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bag to the starting position along the same path, aggressively push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Driving your hips forward is key in this movement as that will help to give you lift from the ground. Just before you reach a standing position, jump as high as you can.
Land softly at your knees by going straight into the next repetition. If you are at the end of your set, remember to allow a bend at the knees to soften the landing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or Kettlebell Squat Jumps.

en: Whilst in a standing position, grab the sandbag and place across your back. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bag to the starting position along the same path, aggressively push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Driving your hips forward is key in this movement as that will help to give you lift from the ground. Just before you reach a standing position, jump as high as you can.
Land softly at your knees by going straight into the next repetition. If you are at the end of your set, remember to allow a bend at the knees to soften the landing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.

es: Mientras estás de pie, toma el sandbag y colócalo sobre tu espalda. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Adopta una postura a la anchura de los hombros con las puntas de los pies ligeramente apuntando hacia afuera. Mantén la espalda recta durante todo el movimiento y mantén la cabeza erguida. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Inhala y comienza a descender doblando las rodillas y las caderas. Su peso debe colocarse predominantemente a través de los talones mientras se lleva la cadera atrás en el movimiento hasta que tus muslos estén paralelos o apenas pasados ​​​​del suelo.

Después de una breve pausa, exhala y regresa el sandbag a la posición inicial siguiendo el mismo camino, empuja agresivamente con los talones y la fuerza se transmite a través de los cuádriceps y los glúteos para hacerlo. Impulsar las caderas hacia adelante es clave en este movimiento, ya que eso ayudará a levantarte del suelo. Justo antes de llegar a la posición de pie, salta lo más alto que puedas.
Aterriza tuavemente sobre tus rodillas y pasa directamente a la siguiente repetición. Si está al final de tu serie, recuerde permitir una flexión de las rodillas para tuavizar el aterrizaje.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Precaución: si sufre problemas de espalda, es posible que este ejercicio no sea adecuado.
Variaciones: saltos de sentadilla con mancuernas o saltos de sentadilla con kettlebells.

name: Sandbag - Burpee Press
instructions: Stand upright holding the sandbag shoulder width apart allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and descend into a press up position. Place the bag on the ground and jump backward with your feet so that your legs are fully extended and your body is supported by your toes and hands.

Exhale and aggressively press through your hands by extending your elbow joints and create enough upward motion to be able to jump your legs back underneath your body so that when you lift the sandbag off the floor, you should be in the bottom of a squat.
Drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

en: Stand upright holding the sandbag shoulder width apart allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and descend into a press up position. Place the bag on the ground and jump backward with your feet so that your legs are fully extended and your body is supported by your toes and hands.

Exhale and aggressively press through your hands by extending your elbow joints and create enough upward motion to be able to jump your legs back underneath your body so that when you lift the sandbag off the floor, you should be in the bottom of a squat.
Drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
es: Colócate de pie en posición vertical sosteniendo el sandbag a la anchura de los hombros, permitiendo que tus brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Inhala y desciende a una posición de flexión hacia arriba. Coloca el sandbag en el suelo y salta hacia atrás con los pies de manera que tus piernas queden completamente extendidas y tu cuerpo quede sostenido por las puntas de los pies y las manos.

Exhala y presione agresivamente con las manos extendiendo las articulaciones de los codos y cree suficiente movimiento hacia arriba para poder saltar con las piernas hacia atrás debajo del cuerpo, de modo que cuando levanta el sandbag del suelo, esté en la parte inferior de una sentadilla.
Conduce con los talones y los cuádriceps para comenzar a elevar el sandbag. Tu espalda debe permanecer recta y tus brazos aún deben estar perpendiculares al suelo en este punto.

Luego viene la aceleración de la pelvis; debes impulsar la pelvis hacia adelante cuando el sandbag llegue a la mitad del muslo. Esto, junto con la extensión a través de las articulaciones de la rodilla y el tobillo, impulsará el sandbag hacia arriba.

Una vez que hayas empujado las caderas hacia adelante, es momento de encogerte de hombros lo más fuerte que puedas para poder levantar más el sandbag. Expanda los codos hacia arriba y hacia afuera hasta que tu cuerpo esté completamente extendido.

Cuando esté en extensión completa, agáchese agresivamente debajo de el sandbag y gire los codos hacia abajo y hacia adentro para que pueda soportar la carga sobre los deltoides frontales (hombros) en una posición de sentadilla frontal. Continúa de sentadilla hasta que se sienta cómodo impulsando hacia arriba los talones, los cuádriceps y los glúteos.
Continúa conduciendo hacia arriba hasta que esté de pie.

Una vez que esté de pie, dobla las rodillas para generar energía y presionar el sandbag sobre tu cabeza hasta que sus codos queden bloqueados. Consejo: Mantener la espalda recta en todo momento es primordial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.

name: Sandbag - Woodchops (Right-Left)
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder width apart holding the sandbag on each side, maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Your first movement will be squatting as you rotate your torso to the right and bring the sandbag diagonally across your body until it is below the hip and next to the right knee.

Exhale and rotate your core so that your hands move up and across your body (in a straight diagonal line). Bringing your arms above your left shoulder. Keep your back and arms straight as you do so. Try to minimise the rotation of your hips so that your core is taking the load.
Once you have turned as far as your torso will allow, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Reverse Cable Woodchops.

en: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder width apart holding the sandbag on each side, maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Your first movement will be squatting as you rotate your torso to the right and bring the sandbag diagonally across your body until it is below the hip and next to the right knee.

Exhale and rotate your core so that your hands move up and across your body (in a straight diagonal line). Bringing your arms above your left shoulder. Keep your back and arms straight as you do so. Try to minimise the rotation of your hips so that your core is taking the load.
Once you have turned as far as your torso will allow, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Reverse Cable Woodchops.
es: Colócate erguido con los pies separados a la anchura de los hombros sosteniendo el sandbag a cada lado, manteniendo la espalda recta y permitiendo que los brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Su primer movimiento será ponerse de sentadilla mientras gira el torso hacia la derecha y lleva el sandbag en diagonal a través de tu cuerpo hasta que esté debajo de la cadera y al lado de la rodilla derecha.

Exhala y gire tu core para que tus manos se muevan hacia arriba y a través de tu cuerpo (en una línea diagonal recta). Llevando los brazos por encima del hombro izquierdo. Mantén la espalda y los brazos rectos mientras lo haces. Trate de minimizar la rotación de tus caderas para que tu core soporte la carga.
Una vez que haya girado tanto como lo deja tu torso, haga una pausa por un breve momento antes de inhalar y regresar los brazos por el mismo camino hasta la posición inicial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones y cambia de lado.
Variación: Sentado en V con rotación de placa o cortes de madera con cable inverso.

name: Sandbag - Woodchops (Left-Right)
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder width apart holding the sandbag on each side, maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Your first movement will be squatting as you rotate your torso to the left and bring the sandbag diagonally across your body until it is below the hip and next to the left knee.

Exhale and rotate your core so that your hands move up and across your body (in a straight diagonal line). Bringing your arms above your right shoulder. Keep your back and arms straight as you do so. Try to minimise the rotation of your hips so that your core is taking the load.
Once you have turned as far as your torso will allow, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Reverse Cable Woodchops.

name: Sandbag - Shoulder To Shoulder Thruster
instructions: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, holding the bag itself and not the handles. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the sandbag by bending at your elbows until the bag is over your right shoulder.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bag back into the starting position and over to your left shoulder by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Sandbag Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.

name.es: Sandbag - Thruster de hombro a hombro

en: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, holding the bag itself and not the handles. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the sandbag by bending at your elbows until the bag is over your right shoulder.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bag back into the starting position and over to your left shoulder by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Sandbag Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.
es: Coge un sandbag y adopta una postura a la anchura de los hombros, sosteniendo el propio sandbag y no las asas. Levanta el sandbag por encima de tu cabeza para que quede directamente por encima de tus hombros. Mantén los codos bloqueados en este punto por seguridad. Mantén la espalda recta durante todo este movimiento. Esta es tu posición inicial.
Mientras inhalas, empieza a bajar el sandbag doblando los codos hasta que el sandbag esté sobre tu hombro derecho.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala y presione el sandbag hacia la posición inicial y hacia tu hombro izquierdo contrayendo los músculos deltoides (hombros).
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Precaución: Las personas con problemas de espalda puedesn encontrar más cómoda la versión sentada.
Variaciones: Press de hombros con sandbag o Press de hombros con máquina Smith.

name: Sandbag - Shoulder To Shoulder
instructions: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width stance with your feet resting the sandbag on your right shoulder holding it in the middle with both hands. This will be your starting position.
Bend down slightly then as you spring up extending through your knees and ankle joints propel the sandbag upward off of your right shoulder over to the left and slowly lowering.
Maintain a straight back throughout this movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

en: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width stance with your feet resting the sandbag on your right shoulder holding it in the middle with both hands. This will be your starting position.
Bend down slightly then as you spring up extending through your knees and ankle joints propel the sandbag upward off of your right shoulder over to the left and slowly lowering.
Maintain a straight back throughout this movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
es: Coge un sandbag y adopta una postura a la anchura de los hombros con los pies apoyando el sandbag en tu hombro derecho sujetándolo por el medio con ambas manos. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Inclínese ligeramente hacia abajo y, a medida que salta, se extiende a través de las rodillas y las articulaciones de los tobillos, impulse el sandbag hacia arriba desde tu hombro derecho hacia el izquierdo y bájelo lentamente.
Mantén la espalda recta durante todo este movimiento.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.

name: Sandbag - Clean & Press
instructions: Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bag is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the sandbag back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure to keep the bag close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Clean & Jerk or Barbell Cleans.

en: Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bag is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the sandbag back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure to keep the bag close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Clean & Jerk or Barbell Cleans.
es: Coge un sandbag con un anchura de los hombros por encima de la empuñadura. Dobla las rodillas y las caderas (más las caderas que las rodillas), manteniendo la espalda recta y permitiendo que los brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo hasta que el sandbag esté justo debajo de las rodillas. En este punto, tu pecho, hombros y cabeza deben mirar hacia adelante. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Tu primer movimiento será impulsar los talones y los cuádriceps para comenzar a elevar el sandbag. Tu espalda debe permanecer recta y tus brazos aún deben estar perpendiculares al suelo en este punto.

Luego viene la aceleración de la pelvis; debes impulsar la pelvis hacia adelante cuando el sandbag llegue a la mitad del muslo. Esto, junto con la extensión a través de las articulaciones de la rodilla y el tobillo, impulsará el sandbag hacia arriba.

Una vez que hayas empujado las caderas hacia adelante, es momento de encogerte de hombros lo más fuerte que puedas para poder levantar más el sandbag. Expanda los codos hacia arriba y hacia afuera hasta que tu cuerpo esté completamente extendido.

Cuando esté en extensión completa, agáchese agresivamente debajo de el sandbag y gire los codos hacia abajo y hacia adentro para que pueda soportar la carga sobre los deltoides frontales (hombros) en una posición de sentadilla frontal. Continúa de sentadilla hasta que se sienta cómodo impulsando hacia arriba los talones, los cuádriceps y los glúteos.
Continúa conduciendo hacia arriba hasta que esté de pie.

Una vez que esté de pie, dobla las rodillas para generar energía y presionar el sandbag sobre tu cabeza hasta que sus codos queden bloqueados. Consejo: Mantener la espalda recta en todo momento es primordial.
Baje el sandbag nuevamente a la posición inicial invirtiendo este movimiento, asegurándose de mantener el sandbag cerca de tu cuerpo en todo momento y manteniendo una buena postura.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Barbell Clean & Jerk o Barbell Cleans.

name: Sandbag - Overhead Press
instructions: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, overhand grip. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the sandbag by bending at your elbows until the bag is below your chin.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bag back into the starting position by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.

name.es: Sandbag - Press por encima de la cabeza

en: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, overhand grip. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.

es: Coge un sandbag y adopta un agarre prono a la anchura de los hombros. Levanta el sandbag por encima de tu cabeza para que quede directamente por encima de tus hombros. Mantén los codos bloqueados en este punto por seguridad. Mantén la espalda recta durante todo este movimiento. Esta es tu posición inicial.
Mientras inhalas, empieza a bajar la sandbag doblando los codos hasta que el sandbag quede debajo de la barbilla.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala y presione el sandbag nuevamente a la posición inicial contrayendo los músculos deltoides (hombros).
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Precaución: Las personas con problemas de espalda puedesn encontrar más cómoda la versión sentada.
Variaciones: Press de hombros con mancuernas o Press de hombros con máquina Smith.

name: Sandbag - Overhead Squats
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name.es: Sandbag - Sentadillas por encima de la cabeza

en: Stand up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.
es: Párate derecho con el sandbag sobre tu cabeza. Esta es tu posición inicial.
Adopta una postura a la anchura de los hombros con las puntas de los pies ligeramente apuntando hacia afuera. Mantén la espalda recta durante todo el movimiento y mantén la cabeza erguida.

Inhala y comienza a descender doblando las rodillas y las caderas. Su peso debe colocarse predominantemente a través de los talones mientras se lleva la cadera atrás en el movimiento hasta que tus muslos estén paralelos o apenas pasados ​​​​del suelo.

Después de una breve pausa, exhala y regresa el sandbag a la posición inicial por el mismo camino, empuja con los talones con la fuerza impulsada a través de tus cuádriceps para hacerlo. Colócate de pie firme con las piernas completamente extendidas, la espalda recta y los hombros relajados.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: sentadillas con barra o sentadillas con kettlebells.

name: Sandbag - Squats (Back)
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed across your back. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Sandbag - Front Squats
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed across your chest. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

name: Sandbag - Zercher Squats
instructions: Stand up straight and place the sandbag so that it is resting on top of your forearms. This will be your starting position.
Position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.

Begin to lower the sandbag by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the sandbag as you exhale by pushing the floor with the ball of your foot mainly as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your arms when lifting the barbell. It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form. Also, make sure that your back is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.

en: Stand up straight and place the sandbag so that it is resting on top of your forearms. This will be your starting position.
Position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.

Begin to lower the sandbag by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Caution: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your arms when lifting the barbell. It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form. Also, make sure that your back is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.
es: Párate derecho y coloca el sandbag de manera que descanse sobre tus antebrazos. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Coloca las piernas en una postura media a la anchura de los hombros y con las puntas de los pies ligeramente apuntados. Mantén la cabeza erguida en todo momento ya que mirar hacia abajo te hará perder el equilibrio y además mantendrás la espalda recta.

Comienza a bajar el sandbag doblando las rodillas mientras mantieness una postura recta con la cabeza erguida. Continúa hacia abajo hasta que el ángulo entre la parte superior de la pierna y las pantorrillas sea ligeramente inferior a 90 grados (que es el punto en el que la parte superior de las piernas queda paralela al suelo). Inhala mientras realizas esta parte del movimiento. Consejo: Si realizaste el ejercicio correctamente, la parte delantera de las rodillas debe formar una línea recta imaginaria con las puntas de los pies que sea perpendicular al frente. Si tus rodillas pasan esa línea imaginaria (si pasan las puntas de los pies), entonces está ejerciendo una tensión indebida sobre la rodilla y el ejercicio se ha realizado incorrectamente.
Comienza a levantar el sandbag mientras exhalas empujando el suelo con la punta del pie principalmente mientras vuelves a estirar las piernas y vuelves a la posición inicial.
Repite el número recomendado de repeticiones.

Precaución: este ejercicio no debe tomarse a la ligera ya que se aplica mucha presión en los brazos al levantar la barra. Siempre es una buena idea tener un observador, ya que puedes ayudarle a mantener la buena técnica. Además, asegúrate de mantener la espalda recta, ya que doblarla puedes provocar lesiones.

name: Sandbag - Squat To Overhead Press
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed across your chest. This will be your starting position.
Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return to the starting position where you will then extend the sandbag above your head and hold.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name.es: Sandbag: sentadillas y press por encima de la cabeza

en: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed across your chest. This will be your starting position.
Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.
