name: Overhead Tricep Extensions Rope
instructions: Attach a rope to a low cable pulley, take hold of the rope and stand with an upright, shoulder width stance facing away from the machine, arms straight above your head with your little fingers on the rubber ends to the rope. Keep your elbows tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the ceiling. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbows pinned in close to your ears. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the rope down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbows are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then exhale and return the rope along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Skullcrushers or Bench Dips.

name.es: Por encima de la cabeza tríceps Extensions Rope

en: Attach a rope to a low cable pulley, take hold of the rope and stand with an upright, shoulder width stance facing away from the machine, arms straight above your head with your little fingers on the rubber ends to the rope. Keep your elbows tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the ceiling. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbows pinned in close to your ears. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the rope down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbows are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then exhale and return the rope along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Skullcrushers or Bench Dips.
es: Sujeta a rope to a low polea pulley, take hold of the rope and stand with an upright, hombro width stance facing away from the máquina, brazos straight above your cabeza with your little fingers on the rubber ends to the rope. Mantén your codos tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the techo. Consejo: Mantén a straight espalda throughout this movimiento and keep your codos pinned in close to your ears. Esta será la posición inicial.
Whilst your upper brazos remain stationary, inspira and lower the rope down on an arcing motion behind your cabeza until your codos are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then espira and return the rope along the same path to the posición inicial mientras contracting your tríceps.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Skullcrushers or Bench Dips.

name: Cable Reverse Crunch
instructions: Attach two handles to a low cable pulley and place a mat in front of it. Lay down on your back and securely put the handles over your toes.

Lift your feet off the ground so that your knees and hips are both at 90 degrees and place your hands on your temples. Tip: Try not to pull your neck or head in to the movement with your hands. If your neck starts to ache, put one hand behind your head for support and to take some weight.
Now exhale and whilst crunching your up torso upward, bring your knees in towards your chest.
Once your abs are as tight as they can be pause for a brief moment, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Cocoons or Jack-Knives.

en: Attach two handles to a low cable pulley and place a mat in front of it. Lay down on your back and securely put the handles over your toes.

Lift your feet off the ground so that your knees and hips are both at 90 degrees and place your hands on your temples. Tip: Try not to pull your neck or head in to the movement with your hands. If your neck starts to ache, put one hand behind your head for support and to take some weight.
Now exhale and whilst crunching your up torso upward, bring your knees in towards your chest.
Once your abs are as tight as they can be pause for a brief moment, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Cocoons or Jack-Knives.
es: Sujeta two asas to a low polea pulley and place a mat in front of it. Túmbate down on your espalda and securely put the asas over your toes.

Lift your pies off the ground so that your rodillas and caderas are both at 90 degrees and place your manos on your temples. Consejo: Try not to pull your neck or cabeza in to the movimiento with your manos. If your neck starts to ache, put one mano behind your cabeza for support and to take some peso.
Now espira and mientras crunching your up torso upward, bring your rodillas in hacia your pecho.
Once your abs are as tight as they can be pause for a brief moment, inspira and return your body espalda along the same path into the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variación: Cocoons or Jack-Knives.

name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
instructions: Remove the handles from two parallel cables. Adjust the height of the pulleys so they are above your head and adopt a standing, shoulder width stance between them.

Once you have done this take hold of the left cable with your right hand and the right cable with your left hand. Your arms should be at full extension with a slight bend at your elbows. This will be your starting position.

Now exhale and pull the cables back (crossing the cables over and keeping them around shoulder height) on a wide arcing motion. Tip: You should be leaning backward to help lift the weights nor should your back come out of a straight position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rear Delt Flyes or Rear Delt Flyes (Machine).

en: Remove the handles from two parallel cables. Adjust the height of the pulleys so they are above your head and adopt a standing, shoulder width stance between them.

Once you have done this take hold of the left cable with your right hand and the right cable with your left hand. Your arms should be at full extension with a slight bend at your elbows. This will be your starting position.

Now exhale and pull the cables back (crossing the cables over and keeping them around shoulder height) on a wide arcing motion. Tip: You should be leaning backward to help lift the weights nor should your back come out of a straight position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rear Delt Flyes or Rear Delt Flyes (Machine).
es: Remove the asas from two parallel poleas. Adjust the height of the pulleys so they are above your cabeza and adopt a standing, hombro width stance between them.

Once you have done this take hold of the left polea with your right mano and the right polea with your left mano. Your brazos should be at full extension with a slight bend at your codos. Esta será la posición inicial.

Now espira and pull the poleas espalda (crossing the poleas over and keeping them around hombro height) on a wide arcing motion. Consejo: You should be leaning espaldaward to help lift the pesos nor should your espalda come out of a straight posición.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return your manos to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Seated Rear Delt Flyes or Rear Delt Flyes (Machine).

name: Straight Arm Pulldowns Rope
instructions: Attach a bar to a high cable pulley, adopt a shoulder width, upright stance and take hold of the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Take a step backwards (around 2 feet or so) and bend slightly forward from your waist whilst maintaining a straight back. Pull the weight down with straight arms so that they are now parallel with the floor. Tip: Keep a slight bend at your elbows throughout. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and pull the bar downwards towards your thighs whilst maintaining both your posture and straight arms. Contract your lats to make this movement happen. Continue to pull the bar downwards towards your thighs until the bar is just a few centimetres away from them.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Dumbbell Pull Overs or Bent Arm Barbell Pullover.

en: Attach a bar to a high cable pulley, adopt a shoulder width, upright stance and take hold of the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Take a step backwards (around 2 feet or so) and bend slightly forward from your waist whilst maintaining a straight back. Pull the weight down with straight arms so that they are now parallel with the floor. Tip: Keep a slight bend at your elbows throughout. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and pull the bar downwards towards your thighs whilst maintaining both your posture and straight arms. Contract your lats to make this movement happen. Continue to pull the bar downwards towards your thighs until the bar is just a few centimetres away from them.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Dumbbell Pull Overs or Bent Arm Barbell Pullover.
es: Sujeta a barra to a high polea pulley, adopt a hombro width, upright stance and take hold of the barra with an overmano grip (palms facing down). Take a step espaldawards (around 2 pies or so) and bend slightly forward from your waist mientras maintaining a straight espalda. Pull the peso down with straight brazos so that they are now parallel with the suelo. Consejo: Mantén a slight bend at your codos throughout. Esta será la posición inicial.

Espira and pull the barra downwards hacia your thighs mientras maintaining both your posture and straight brazos. Contract your dorsales to make this movimiento happen. Continue to pull the barra downwards hacia your thighs until the barra is just a few centimetres away from them.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return the barra along the same path espalda to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variación: mancuerna Pull Overs or Bent Arm barra Pullover.

name: Cable Preacher Curls
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench facing a low cable pulley fitted with a straight or EZ bar attachment. Rest the back of your upper arms on the pad and grasp the bar with an underhand (supinated) grip, arms fully extended — this is the start position.
Exhale and curl the bar up by contracting the biceps, keeping your elbows stationary on the pad. Lift until your forearms are near vertical and squeeze at the top.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the bar under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions. Variation: use a single-handle attachment for unilateral cable preacher curls or perform with an EZ bar on a dedicated preacher-machine.

en: Sit on a preacher bench facing a low cable pulley fitted with a straight or EZ bar attachment. Rest the back of your upper arms on the pad and grasp the bar with an underhand (supinated) grip, arms fully extended — this is the start position.
Exhale and curl the bar up by contracting the biceps, keeping your elbows stationary on the pad. Lift until your forearms are near vertical and squeeze at the top.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the bar under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions. Variation: use a single-handle attachment for unilateral cable preacher curls or perform with an EZ bar on a dedicated preacher-machine.
es: Siéntate en un banco predicador frente a una polea baja equipada con una barra recta o EZ. Apoya la parte posterior de los brazos sobre el relleno y sujeta la barra con agarre supinado (palmas hacia arriba), con los brazos extendidos —esta es la posición inicial.

Espira y flexiona los codos contrayendo los bíceps para elevar la barra, manteniendo los codos fijos sobre el acolchado. Eleva hasta que los antebrazos queden casi verticales y aprieta los bíceps en la parte alta.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira y baja la barra de forma controlada hasta la posición inicial, con los brazos completamente extendidos.
Repite las repeticiones indicadas. Variación: usa un mango individual para trabajar de forma unilateral o realiza el curl predicador con barra EZ en una máquina predicador.

name: Single Arm Cable Tricep Extensions
instructions: Attach a handle to a high cable pulley. Adopt a shoulder width, upright stance (about 18 inches away from the machine) and take hold of the handle with an overhand grip (palm facing the floor).

Once you have hold of the handle, take the weight by tucking your elbow into the side of your torso. Your forearm and upper arm should create an acute angle (less than 90 degrees) when ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and push the handle down towards your side with an arcing motion. Use your tricep to do this and contract fully when your arm is by your side. At this point, your arm should be perpendicular to the floor. Tip: Keep your elbow tucked in to your side as only the forearm should move.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extensions.

name.es: A una mano polea tríceps Extensions

en: Attach a handle to a high cable pulley. Adopt a shoulder width, upright stance (about 18 inches away from the machine) and take hold of the handle with an overhand grip (palm facing the floor).

Once you have hold of the handle, take the weight by tucking your elbow into the side of your torso. Your forearm and upper arm should create an acute angle (less than 90 degrees) when ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and push the handle down towards your side with an arcing motion. Use your tricep to do this and contract fully when your arm is by your side. At this point, your arm should be perpendicular to the floor. Tip: Keep your elbow tucked in to your side as only the forearm should move.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extensions.
es: Sujeta a asa to a high polea pulley. Adopt a hombro width, upright stance (about 18 inches away from the máquina) and take hold of the asa with an overmano grip (palm facing the suelo).

Once you have hold of the asa, take the peso by tucking your codo into the side of your torso. Your forebrazo and upper brazo should create an acute angle (less than 90 degrees) when ready to go. Esta será la posición inicial.

To initiate the movimiento, espira and push the asa down hacia your side with an arcing motion. Use your tricep to do this and contract fully when your brazo is by your side. At this point, your brazo should be perpendicular to the suelo. Consejo: Mantén your codo tucked in to your side as only the forebrazo should move.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return the asa along the same path espalda to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado and swap brazos.
Variaciones: Tricep Kickespaldas or Tricep Rope Extensions.

name: Cable Skull Crushers Rope Attachment
instructions: Place a bar attachment onto a low cable pulley and place a flat bench facing away from it. Lay down on the bench with your head at the cable end. Adopt an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and press the bar straight up towards the ceiling. Keep your elbows as close together as you can to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.
Inhale as you descend the bar towards your forehead. Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your forehead pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extenions.

en: Place a bar attachment onto a low cable pulley and place a flat bench facing away from it. Lay down on the bench with your head at the cable end. Adopt an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and press the bar straight up towards the ceiling. Keep your elbows as close together as you can to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.
Inhale as you descend the bar towards your forehead. Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your forehead pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extenions.
es: Coloca a barra attachment onto a low polea pulley and place a flat banco facing away from it. Túmbate down on the banco with your cabeza at the polea end. Adopt an overmano grip (palms facing away from you) and press the barra straight up hacia the techo. Mantén your codos as close together as you can to initiate maximum tricep activity. Esta será la posición inicial.
Inspira as you descend the barra hacia your forecabeza. Be sure to keep your upper brazos stationary and your codos tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your forecabeza pause for a second.
Then mientras exhaling, use your tríceps to return the barra to the posición inicial. Your brazos should be fully extended once again and pointing hacia the techo.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Tricep Kickespaldas or Tricep Rope Extenions.

name: Cable Lateral Raises
instructions: Place a handle attachment onto a low cable pulley. Stand side on to the machine with your right side being closest. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position, maintaining a straight back throughout. Reach across your body and take hold of the handle with your left hand. Return to your original, standing position. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating your left hand on a wide arc out to your side. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arm is parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arm along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Dumbbell Lateral Raises or Dynaband Lateral Raises.

en: Place a handle attachment onto a low cable pulley. Stand side on to the machine with your right side being closest. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position, maintaining a straight back throughout. Reach across your body and take hold of the handle with your left hand. Return to your original, standing position. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating your left hand on a wide arc out to your side. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arm is parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arm along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Dumbbell Lateral Raises or Dynaband Lateral Raises.
es: Coloca a asa attachment onto a low polea pulley. Colócate de pie side on to the máquina with your right side being closest. Adopt a hombro width, upright standing posición, maintaining a straight espalda throughout. Reach across your body and take hold of the asa with your left mano. Return to your original, standing posición. Esta será la posición inicial.

Initiate the movimiento by exhaling and elevating your left mano on a wide arc out to your side. Mantén a slight bend at your codo and continue to elevate upward until your brazo is parallel with the suelo.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return your brazo along the same path espalda to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado and swap sides.
Variación: mancuerna Lateral Raises or Dynabanda Lateral Raises.

name: Cable Iron Cross
instructions: Place handle attachments on two high parallel cable pulleys. Once you have done this, stand inbetween the cables (facing forward) and grab one in a each hand. Have a shoulder width, upright stance with your back straight and elbows slightly bent. Your head should be up and your chest pushed out. Your position should now resemble a 'T'. This will be your starting position.

Keeping your arms out wide, exhale and bring your hands down towards the middle of your hips on two wide arcing paths. Tip: Your pectoral muscles should be initiating this movement but only your arms should be moving.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands back along the same paths towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Pec Deck.

en: Place handle attachments on two high parallel cable pulleys. Once you have done this, stand inbetween the cables (facing forward) and grab one in a each hand. Have a shoulder width, upright stance with your back straight and elbows slightly bent. Your head should be up and your chest pushed out. Your position should now resemble a 'T'. This will be your starting position.

Keeping your arms out wide, exhale and bring your hands down towards the middle of your hips on two wide arcing paths. Tip: Your pectoral muscles should be initiating this movement but only your arms should be moving.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands back along the same paths towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Pec Deck.
es: Coloca asa attachments on two high parallel polea pulleys. Once you have done this, stand inbetween the poleas (facing forward) and grab one in a each mano. Have a hombro width, upright stance with your espalda straight and codos slightly bent. Your cabeza should be up and your pecho pushed out. Your posición should now resemble a 'T'. Esta será la posición inicial.

Manténing your brazos out wide, espira and bring your manos down hacia the middle of your caderas on two wide arcing paths. Consejo: Your pectoral muscles should be initiating this movimiento but only your brazos should be moving.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return your manos espalda along the same paths hacia the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: mancuerna Flyes or Pec Deck.

name: Cable Internal Rotation
instructions: Attach a handle to the cable and adjust the height of the pulley so that it sits at belly button height. Stand side on to the machine and take a step away. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position and take hold of the handle with the hand nearest the machine. Once you have hold of the handle, tuck your elbow in close to your torso (your elbow should be at a 90 degree angle). This will be your starting position.

Exhale and rotate your hand on an imaginary semicricle in front of you, pivoting from your elbow which, remains tight against the side of your torso. Rotate your shoulder fully so that your hand is close to the opposite side of your body.
After a brief pause, return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Caution: Be cautious with the weight as you could damage your rotator cuff.
Variations: Dumbbell Internal Rotations or Dynaband Internal Rotations

en: Attach a handle to the cable and adjust the height of the pulley so that it sits at belly button height. Stand side on to the machine and take a step away. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position and take hold of the handle with the hand nearest the machine. Once you have hold of the asa, tuck your codo in close to your torso (your codo should be at a 90 degree angle). Esta será la posición inicial.

Espira and rotate your mano on an imaginary semicricle in front of you, pivoting from your codo which, remains tight against the side of your torso. Rotate your hombro fully so that your mano is close to the opposite side of your body.
Tras una breve pausa, return the asa along the same path espalda to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado and swap brazos.
Precaución: Be cautious with the peso as you could damage your rotator cuff.
Variaciones: mancuerna Internal Rotations or Dynabanda Internal Rotations

name: Incline Cable Tricep Extensions V Bar Attachment
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is at the top of its runner and set the weight accordingly. Place an incline bench in front of the cable but facing away from the machine. Next, attach a 'V' bar to the cable and lay on the bench (chest facing the ceiling) that is set to an incline angle of around 30-45 degrees. Extend your arms vertically towards the ceiling and take an overhand grip of the 'V' bar. Your palms should now be facing forwards. Bend your elbows so that they are tucked in and are at a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and extend your arms out and away from your body on a wide arcing motion. Continue to press the weight away from your body until your arms are fully extended and your triceps are fully contracted. Tip: Keep your elbows as close together as possible throughout the movement.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Kickbacks or Tricep Extension (Rope).

name.en: Incline Cable Tricep Extensions V Bar Attachment
name.es: Inclinado polea tríceps Extensions V Bar Attachment

Variations: Dumbbell Kickbacks or Tricep Extension (Rope).

Next, attach a 'V' barra to the polea and lay on the banco (pecho facing the techo) that is set to an incline angle of around 30-45 degrees. Extend your brazos vertically hacia the techo and take an overmano grip of the 'V' barra. Your palms should now be facing forwards. Bend your codos so that they are tucked in and are at a 90 degree angle. Esta será la posición inicial.

To initiate the movimiento, espira and extend your brazos out and away from your body on a wide arcing motion. Continue to press the peso away from your body until your brazos are fully extended and your tríceps are fully contracted. Consejo: Mantén your codos as close together as possible throughout the movimiento.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return the barra along the same path espalda into its posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: mancuerna Kickespaldas or Tricep Extension (Rope).

aliases: Incline Cable Tricep Extensions V Bar Attachment

name: Cable Incline Pushdown
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is at the top of its runner and set the weight accordingly. Place an incline bench in front of the cable but facing away from the machine. Next, attach a 'V' bar to the cable and lay on the bench (chest facing the ceiling) that is set to an incline angle of around 30 degrees. Extend your arms vertically towards the ceiling and take an overhand grip of the 'V' bar. Your palms should now be facing forwards. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your arms down towards your thighs (whilst keeping them extended throughout) on a wide arcing motion. Continue to pull the weight down towards your thighs until your arms are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Pullover or Bent-Arm Barbell Pullover.

name: Cable Hip Adduction
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low pulley. Stand side on to the pulley system and attach the cuff to the ankle nearest the cable.

Once you have done this take a large step away from the cable pulley. Now have your weight on the leg that the cuff is not attached to (the furthest foot away from the machine). If you need to hold onto something for stability, you can do so.
Allow your ankle (which the cable is attached to) to be pulled towards the machine. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and move the weight loaded ankle across your body (away from the machine) and in front of your standing foot using your inner thigh muscles.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the ankle along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap ankles.
Variation: Adductor Machine or Leg Press (Wide Stance).

Once you have done this take a large step away from the cable pulley. Now have your weight on the leg that the cuff is not attached to (the furthest foot away from the machine). If you need to hold onto something for stability, you can do so.

Espira and move the peso loaded ankle across your body (away from the máquina) and in front of your standing pie using your inner thigh muscles.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return the ankle along the same path to the posición inicial.

Variación: Adductor Machine or Leg Press (Wide Stance).

name: Cable Hammer Curls Rope
instructions: Attach a rope to a low cable pulley. Take hold of the ropes with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) making sure that the plastic/rubber part on the end is touching your thumbs and index fingers. Stand upright around 12 inches away from the cable, maintaining a straight back throughout.

Whilst holding the upper arm stationary, exhale as you curl the rope up towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps. Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the the rope is at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze your biceps.
(Tip: Only the forearms should move.)
Inhale as you slowly begin to return the rope along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Hammer Curls or Seated Hammer Curls.

es: Sujeta a rope to a low polea pulley. Take hold of the ropes with a agarre neutro (palms facing each other) making sure that the plastic/rubber part on the end is touching your thumbs and index fingers. Colócate de pie upright around 12 inches away from the polea, maintaining a straight espalda throughout.

Whilst holding the upper brazo stationary, espira as you curl the rope up hacia your hombros by contracting your bíceps. Continue the movimiento until your bíceps are fully contracted and the the rope is at hombro level. Hold the contracted posición for a second as you squeeze your bíceps.
(Consejo: Only the forebrazos should move.)
Inspira as you slowly begin to return the rope along the same path to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Colócate de pieing Hammer Curls or Seated Hammer Curls.

name: Cable Deadlifts
instructions: Adjust two opposing cable pulleys so they are at the bottom of their runners and set the weights accordingly. Position yourself in between these runners with a shoulder width stance. Attach a bar to each cable and (whilst maintaining good posture (a straight back)), bend at your knees and hips to lower your torso, enabling you to take hold of the bar. This will be the starting position of the exercise.

To initiate the movement, exhale and press through your heels and quads whilst simultaneously using the muscles in your back to bring your torso into a vertical position. Once you are in a standing position you should push your chest out by pulling your shoulder blades together.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hex Bar Deadlifts or Barbell Deadlifts.

en: Adjust two opposing cable pulleys so they are at the bottom of their runners and set the weights accordingly. Position yourself in between these runners with a shoulder width stance. Attach a bar to each cable and (whilst maintaining good posture (a straight back)), bend at your knees and hips to lower your torso, enabling you to take hold of the bar. This will be the starting position of the exercise.

To initiate the movement, exhale and press through your heels and quads whilst simultaneously using the muscles in your back to bring your torso into a vertical position. Once you are in a standing position you should push your chest out by pulling your shoulder blades together.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hex Bar Deadlifts or Barbell Deadlifts.
es: Adjust two opposing polea pulleys so they are at the bottom of their runners and set the pesos accordingly. Position yourself in between these runners with a hombro width stance. Sujeta a barra to each polea and (mientras maintaining good posture (a straight espalda)), bend at your rodillas and caderas to lower your torso, enabling you to take hold of the barra. This will be the posición inicial of the exercise.

To initiate the movimiento, espira and press through your talones and cuádriceps mientras simultaneously using the muscles in your espalda to bring your torso into a vertical posición. Once you are in a standing posición you should push your pecho out by pulling your hombro blades together.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return the barra along the same path espalda into its posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Hex Bar Deadlifts or barra Deadlifts.

name: Cable Crunches
instructions: Adopt a kneeling position underneath a high cable with a rope attached. When setting the weight for this exercise, be cautious as if it is too heavy, your abs will not be taking the load and your lower back will play a part.
Take hold of the ropes and bring them down so they are beside your head on your upper chest. Push your hips back and lean forward to create a 90 degree angle from your lower back and thighs.

From here you should try to arch your back by taking a deep breath, pulling your shoulders back and pushing your chest out. This will be your starting position. Tip: Throughout this movement your hips and your lower limbs should remain stationary.

Exhale and flex your abs to move your shoulders (and rope) downward towards your thighs. As you flex your abs, the arch in your back reverses so that your back is now rounding over. Your elbows should remain as close to your torso as possible throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return along the same path to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back pain, be cautious of the resistance.
Variations: Crunches or Cocoons.

Exhale and flex your abs to move your shoulders (and rope) downward towards your thighs. As you flex your abs, the arch in your back reverses so that your back is now rounding over. Your elbows should remain as close to your torso as possible throughout.

es: Adopt a rodillaling posición underneath a high polea with a rope attached. When setting the peso for this exercise, be cautious as if it is too heavy, your abs will not be taking the load and your zona lumbar will play a part.
Take hold of the ropes and bring them down so they are beside your cabeza on your upper pecho. Push your caderas espalda and lean forward to create a 90 degree angle from your zona lumbar and thighs.

From here you should try to arch your espalda by taking a deep breath, pulling your hombros espalda and pushing your pecho out. Esta será la posición inicial. Consejo: Throughout this movimiento your caderas and your lower limbs should remain stationary.

Espira and flex your abs to move your hombros (and rope) downward hacia your thighs. As you flex your abs, the arch in your espalda reverses so that your espalda is now rounding over. Your codos should remain as close to your torso as possible throughout.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return along the same path to your posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Precaución: If you suffer from zona lumbar pain, be cautious of the resistencia.
Variaciones: Crunches or Cocoons.

name: Cable Chest Press
instructions: Attach handles to two cables (placed at shoulder height). Take one in each hand and face away from the machine. Bring the handles up to shoulder height with your elbows parallel with the floor, flared out wide at a 90 degree angle. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance, maintaining a straight back troughout the movement. Your palms should be facing the floor. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press the handles in front of you until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight) and bring your hands together.
After a brief pause and squeeze of your pectoral muscles, return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Chest Press or Chest Press Machine

es: Sujeta asas to two poleas (placed at hombro height). Take one in each mano and face away from the máquina. Bring the asas up to hombro height with your codos parallel with the suelo, flared out wide at a 90 degree angle. Adopt a standing, hombro width stance, maintaining a straight espalda troughout the movimiento. Your palms should be facing the suelo. Esta será la posición inicial.
Whilst exhaling, press the asas in front of you until your codo becomes fully extended (straight) and bring your manos together.
Tras una breve pausa and squeeze of your pectoral muscles, return the asas espalda along the same path to the posición inicial.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variaciones: mancuerna Chest Press or Chest Press Machine

name: Bent Over Single Arm Low Cable Rear Delt Flyes
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is at the bottom of its runner with a handle attached. Stand with your right side adjacent to the runner and take hold of the handle with an underhand grip in your left hand. Bend at your knees and waist (more so at your waist) whilst maintaining a straight back so that your torso is just off parallel with the floor. Your right arm should be in a neutral position so that it does not interfere with the movement. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the back of your left hand up and out, on a wide arcing motion, away from the pulley and towards the ceiling. Continue to pull until your arm is parallel to the floor. To do so, use the muscles in the back of your shoulder joint and across the top of your back. Tip: Maintain your posture throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Rear Delt Machine Flyes or Face Pulls.

Your right brazo should be in a neutral posición so that it does not interfere with the movimiento. Esta es la posición inicial.

To initiate the movimiento, espira and pull the espalda of your left mano up and out, on a wide arcing motion, away from the pulley and hacia the techo. Continue to pull until your brazo is parallel to the suelo. To do so, use the muscles in the espalda of your hombro joint and across the top of your espalda. Consejo: Mantén your posture throughout.

name: Standing Alternating Cable Shoulder Press
instructions: Attach handles to two low cables. Take one in each hand and face away from the machine. Bring the handles up to shoulder height with your elbows are flared out wide and at a 90 degree angle. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance, maintaining a straight back troughout the movement. Your palms should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press one of the handles towards the ceiling until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight). Keep your other arm stationary in the starting position.
After a brief pause, return the handle back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the opposite side and alternate between arms.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Alternating Shoulder Press or See-Saw Press.

name: Bodyweight Flyes
instructions: Position two equally loaded barbells on the ground next to each other. Ensure they are able to roll.
Assume a push-up position over the bars, supporting your weight on your toes and hands with your arms extended and body straight.
Place your hands on the bars. This will be your starting position.
Using a slow and controlled motion, move your hands away from the midline of your body, rolling the bars apart. Inhale during this portion of the motion.
After moving the bars as far apart as you can, return to the starting position by pulling them back together. Exhale as you perform this movement.

name: Sumo Stiff Legged Deadlifts
instructions: Place a loaded barbell in front of you and adopt a wide stance. Have your shins gently touching the barbell, lean forward from your hips to take an overhand, narrow grip (index fingers 6-12 inches apart). Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor, a slight bend at your knees and remember to maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your knees stationary, exhale as you drive through your heels to elevate the barbell. You should be pulling through your hamstrings and driving your hips forward at the same time to lift the bar up into a standing position.

After a brief pause in this standing position, lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the barbell close to you as you go. Push your glutes back to help lower the bar towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Inhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variation: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Deadlifts.

Whilst keeping your rodillas stationary, espira as you drive through your talones to elevate the barrara. You should be pulling through your isquios and driving your caderas forward at the same time to lift the barra up into a standing posición.

Tras una breve pausa in this standing posición, lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight espalda), keeping the barrara close to you as you go. Push your glúteos espalda to help lower the barra hacia your pies until you feel a stretch in your isquios. Consejo: Remember, this whole movimiento is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the rodilla, but no more) and a straight espalda throughout. Inspira as you perform this part of the movimiento.

Precaución: This is not an exercise for those with espalda issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.

name: Wide-Stance Barbell Squat
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across your shoulders whilst maintaining a straight back and a small bend at your knees. Press through your legs to lift the bar and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Wide-Grip Decline Barbell Pullover
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with both legs securely locked in position.  Reach for the barbell behind the head using a pronated grip (palms facing out).  Make sure to grab the barbell wider than shoulder width apart for this exercise.  Slowly lift the barbell up from the floor by using your arms.
When positioned properly, your arms should be fully extended and perpendicular to the floor.  This is the starting position.

Begin by moving the barbell back down in a semicircular motion as if you were going to place it on the floor, but instead, stop when the arms are parallel to the floor.  Tip: Keep the arms fully extended at all times.  The movement should only happen at the shoulder joint.  Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Now bring the barbell up while exhaling until you are back at the starting position.  Remember to keep full control of the barbell at all times.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions of your training program.
When finished with your set, slowly lower the barbell back down until it is level with your head and release it.
Caution: It is a good idea to have a spotter for this exercise.
Variations: You can also use an EZ bar or dumbbells to perform this movement.

Make sure to grab the barbell wider than shoulder width apart for this exercise.  Slowly lift the barbell up from the floor by using your arms.

Empieza by moving the barrara espalda down in a semicircular motion as if you were going to place it on the suelo, but instead, stop when the brazos are parallel to the suelo. Variaciones: You can also use an EZ barra or mancuernas to perform this movimiento.

name: Wide-Grip Decline Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie back on a decline bench with the feet securely locked at the front of the bench.  Using a wide, pronated (palms forward) grip that is around 3 inches away from shoulder width (for each hand), lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with your arms locked.  The bar will be perpendicular to the torso and the floor.  This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the bar on your lower chest.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your chest muscles.  Lock your arms and squeeze your chest in the contracted position, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again.  Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Caution:
If you are new at this exercise, it is advised that you use a spotter.  If no spotter is available, then be conservative with the amount of weight used.
Also, beware of letting the bar drift too far forward.  You want the bar to fall on your middle chest and nowhere else.
Don't bounce the weight off your chest.  You should be in full control of the barbell at all times.
Variations: You can use dumbbells for this exercise as well as exercise bands.
Same as the Decline Barbell Bench Press but with a wider grip.

name.es: Wide-Grip declinado barra press de banca

Lock your arms and squeeze your chest in the contracted position, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again.  Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Caution:
If you are new at this exercise, it is advised that you use a spotter.  If no spotter is available, then be conservative with the amount of weight used.
Also, beware of letting the bar drift too far forward.  You want the bar to fall on your middle chest and nowhere else.
Don't bounce the weight off your chest.  You should be in full control of the barbell at all times.
Variations: You can use dumbbells for this exercise as well as exercise bands.
Same as the Decline Barbell Bench Press but with a wider grip.
es: Túmbate espalda on a decline banco with the pies securely locked at the front of the banco.  Using a wide, pronated (palms forward) grip that is around 3 inches away from hombro width (for each mano), lift the barra from the rack and hold it straight over you with your brazos locked.  The barra will be perpendicular to the torso and the suelo.  Esta será la posición inicial.
As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the barra on your lower pecho.

After a second pause, bring the barra espalda to the posición inicial as you breathe out and push the barra using your pecho muscles.  Lock your brazos and squeeze your pecho in the contracted posición, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again.  Consejo: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movimiento for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Precaución:
If you are new at this exercise, it is advised that you use a spotter.  If no spotter is available, then be conservative with the amount of peso used.
Also, beware of letting the barra drift too far forward.  You want the barra to fall on your middle pecho and nowhere else.
Don't bounce the peso off your pecho.  You should be in full control of the barrara at all times.
Variaciones: You can use mancuernas for this exercise as well as exercise bandas.
Same as the Decline barra Bench Press but with a wider grip.

name: Wide-Grip Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie back on a flat bench with feet firm on the floor.  Using a wide, pronated (palms forward) grip that is around 3 inches away from shoulder width (for each hand), lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with your arms locked.  The bar will be perpendicular to the torso and the floor.  This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the bar on your middle chest.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your chest muscles.  Lock your arms and squeeze your chest in the contracted position, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again.  Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Caution:
If you are new at this exercise, it is advised that you use a spotter.  If no spotter is available, then be conservative with the amount of weight used.
Also, beware of letting the bar drift too far forward.  You want the bar to fall on your middle chest and nowhere else.
Don't bounce the weight off your chest.  You should be in full control of the barbell at all times.
Variations: You can use dumbbells for this exercise as well as exercise bands.
Same as the Barbell Bench Press but with a different grip.

name: Alternating Barbell Lunges
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.
Step underneath the bar so that it is resting on your shoulders. Take hold of the bar and step back from the rack maintaining a straight back throughout.This will be your starting position.

Inahle and take a slightly exaggerated step forwardand plant your foot firmly on the floor. Whilst maintaining a stragiht back descend your torso towards the floor by bending both knees into a 90 degree position. Tip: Keep your weight central and drop directly downward rather than forward or back, your knees should be at right angles when at the bottom of the movement.
After a brief pause press through your front thigh and heel to push your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Alternate in this fashion and repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you have any balance issues, you should use an alternative method of adding weight to this exercise. For example, use dumbbells.
Variations: Alternating Dumbbell Lunges or Dumbbell Walking Lunges.

es: Coloca the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barrara can lay across your hombros comfortably. Next, set the safety barras at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the peso and you will be safe. Load up the barrara with the correct amount of peso on it and you are now ready to go.
Step underneath the barra so that it is resting on your hombros. Take hold of the barra and step espalda from the rack maintaining a straight espalda throughout.Esta será la posición inicial.

Inahle and take a slightly exaggerated step forwardand plant your pie firmly on the suelo. Whilst maintaining a stragiht espalda descend your torso hacia the suelo by bending both rodillas into a 90 degree posición. Consejo: Mantén your peso central and drop directly downward rather than forward or espalda, your rodillas should be at right angles when at the bottom of the movimiento.
Tras una breve pausa press through your front thigh and talón to push your body espalda along the same path into the posición inicial.
Alternate in this fashion and repeat the movimiento for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Precaución: If you have any balance issues, you should use an alternative method of adding peso to this exercise. For example, use mancuernas.
Variaciones: Alternating mancuerna Lunges or mancuerna Walking Lunges.

name: Squat Jumps (Barbell)
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across your shoulders whilst maintaining a straight back and a small bend at your knees. Press through your legs to lift the bar and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, aggressively push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Driving your hips forward is key in this movement as that will help to give you lift from the ground. Just before you reach a standing position, jump as high as you can.
Land softly at your knees by going straight into the next repetition. If you are at the end of your set, remember to allow a bend at the knees to soften the landing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or Kettlebell Squat Jumps.

es: Coloca the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barrara can lay across your hombros comfortably. Next, set the safety barras at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the peso and you will be safe. Load up the barrara with the correct amount of peso on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the barra and take the peso across your hombros mientras maintaining a straight espalda and a small bend at your rodillas. Press through your legs to lift the barra and take a small step espaldawards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a hombro width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Mantén a straight espalda throughout the movimiento and keep your cabeza up. Esta será la posición inicial.

Inspira and begin to descend by bending at your rodillas and caderas. Your peso should be put predominantly through your talones as you sit espalda into the movimiento until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the suelo.

Tras una breve pausa, espira and return the barra to the posición inicial along the same path, aggressively push through your talones with the force being driven through your cuádriceps and glúteos to do so. Driving your caderas forward is key in this movimiento as that will help to give you lift from the ground. Just before you reach a standing posición, jump as high as you can.
Land softly at your rodillas by going straight into the next repetition. If you are at the end of your set, remember to allow a bend at the rodillas to soften the landing.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Precaución: If you suffer from espalda issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variaciones: mancuerna Squat Jumps or Kettlebell Squat Jumps.

name: Upright Barbell Rows
instructions: Grab a barbell with a narrow, overhand grip. Adopt a standing position with the barbell resting against your thighs, your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the bar as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the barbell up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the barbell upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bar.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the barbell along the same path towards the starting position.
Variations: Dumbbell Upright Rows or Smith Machine Upright Row

es: Coge a barrara with a narrow, overmano grip. Adopt a standing posición with the barrara resting against your thighs, your brazos hanging perpendicular to the suelo. Esta será la posición inicial. Consejo: Mantén a straight espalda and keep the barra as close to your body as possible throughout the movimiento.

Espira and pull the barrara up hacia your chin by contracting your hombros and flaring your codos out wide. Continue to pull the barrara upward until you reach your chin. At this point your codos should be above the barra.
Pause for a brief moment, then inspira and return the barrara along the same path hacia the posición inicial.
Variaciones: mancuerna Upright Rows or multipower Upright Row

name: Landmine T Bar Rows
instructions: Load up the T-Bar barbell with the desired amount of weight and take your stance either side of the bar. Take hold of the handles with a wider than shoulder width, overhand grip by bending at your knees and waist (more so at your waist) whilst maintaining a straight back. Your arms should be hanging perpendicular to the floor at this point. This is your starting position.
Exhale and squeeze the muscles in your back to bring the barbell in towards your torso. Bend at the elbow to allow the movement but try not to take any weight through your forearms/ biceps.
Once the barbell is as close to your body as it can be or the muscles in your back are as tight as they can be, inhale and return the barbell along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This exercise may not be suitable for people that suffer from any back pain/injuries.
Variations: Bent Over Barbell Row or Seated Row Machine (Overhand Grip).

name: Svend Press
instructions: Begin in a standing position.

Press two lightweight plates together with your hands. Hold the plates together close to your chest to create an isometric contraction in your chest muscles. Your fingers should be pointed forward. This is your starting position.
Squeeze the plates between your palms and extend your arms directly out in front of you in a controlled motion.
Pause at the top of the motion, and then slowly return to the starting position.

es: Empieza in a standing posición.

Press two lightpeso plates together with your manos. Hold the plates together close to your pecho to create an isometric contraction in your pecho muscles. Your fingers should be pointed forward. Esta es la posición inicial.
Squeeze the plates between your palms and extend your brazos directly out in front of you in a controlled motion.
Pause at the top of the motion, and then slowly return to the posición inicial.

name: Stiff Leg Deadlifts (Sumo Stance)
instructions: Place a loaded barbell in front of you and adopt a wide stance. Have your shins gently touching the barbell, lean forward from your hips to take an overhand, narrow grip (index fingers 6-12 inches apart). Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor, a slight bend at your knees and remember to maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your knees stationary, exhale as you drive through your heels to elevate the barbell. You should be pulling through your hamstrings and driving your hips forward at the same time to lift the bar up into a standing position.

After a brief pause in this standing position, lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the barbell close to you as you go. Push your glutes back to help lower the bar towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Inhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variation: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Deadlifts.

name: Sumo Squats (Barbell)
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across your shoulders whilst maintaining a straight back and a small bend at your knees. Press through your legs to lift the bar and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Chained Sumo Deadlifts
instructions: You can attach the chains to the sleeves of the bar, or just drape the middle over the bar so there is a greater weight increase as you lift. Attempt to keep the ends of the chains away from the plates so you don't hit them when you lower the weight.

Begin with a bar loaded on the ground. Approach the bar so that the bar intersects the middle of the feet. The feet should be set very wide, near the collars. Bend at the hips to grip the bar. The arms should be directly below the shoulders, inside the legs, and you can use a pronated grip, a mixed grip, or hook grip. Relax the shoulders, which in effect lengthens your arms.

Take a breath, and then lower your hips, looking forward with your head with your chest up. Drive through the floor, spreading your feet apart, with your weight on the back half of your feet. Extend through the hips and knees.
As the bar passes through the knees, lean back and drive the hips into the bar, pulling your shoulder blades together.
Return the weight to the ground by bending at the hips and controlling the weight on the way down.

en: You can attach the chains to the sleeves of the bar, or just drape the middle over the bar so there is a greater weight increase as you lift. Attempt to keep the ends of the chains away from the plates so you don't hit them when you lower the weight.

The arms should be directly below the shoulders, inside the legs, and you can use a pronated grip, a mixed grip, or hook grip. Relax the shoulders, which in effect lengthens your arms.

Take a breath, and then lower your hips, looking forward with your head with your chest up. Drive through the floor, spreading your feet apart, with your weight on the back half of your feet. Extend through the hips and knees.
As the bar passes through the knees, lean back and drive the hips into the bar, pulling your shoulder blades together.
Return the weight to the ground by bending at the hips and controlling the weight on the way down.
es: You can attach the chains to the sleeves of the barra, or just drape the middle over the barra so there is a greater peso increase as you lift. Attempt to keep the ends of the chains away from the plates so you don't hit them when you lower the peso.

Empieza with a barra loaded on the ground. Approach the barra so that the barra intersects the middle of the pies. The pies should be set very wide, near the collars. Bend at the caderas to grip the barra. The brazos should be directly below the hombros, inside the legs, and you can use a pronated grip, a mixed grip, or hook grip. Relax the hombros, which in effect lengthens your brazos.

Take a breath, and then lower your caderas, looking forward with your cabeza with your pecho up. Drive through the suelo, spreading your pies apart, with your peso on the espalda half of your pies. Extend through the caderas and rodillas.
As the barra passes through the rodillas, lean espalda and drive the caderas into the barra, pulling your hombro blades together.
Return the peso to the ground by bending at the caderas and controlling the peso on the way down.

name: Sumo Deadlift with Bands
instructions: To deadlift with short bands, simply loop them over the bar before you start, and step into them to set up.  Ensure that they under the back half of your foot, directly where you are driving into the floor.

Begin with a bar loaded on the ground. Approach the bar so that the bar intersects the middle of the feet. The feet should be set very wide, near the collars. Bend at the hips to grip the bar. The arms should be directly below the shoulders, inside the legs, and you can use a pronated grip, a mixed grip, or hook grip.

Take a breath, and then lower your hips, looking forward with your head with your chest up. Drive through the floor, spreading your feet apart, with your weight on the back half of your feet. Extend through the hips and knees.
As the bar passes through the knees, lean back and drive the hips into the bar, pulling your shoulder blades together.
Return the weight to the ground by bending at the hips and controlling the weight on the way down.

name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Load a barbell and stand behind it with a wide stance, toes pointed slightly outward. Your shins should be close to or lightly touching the bar.

Keeping your back flat and chest up, bend at the hips and knees to lower into position and grip the bar inside your knees with a shoulder-width overhand grip. Your hips should sit higher than your knees. A mixed grip (one overhand, one underhand) is also acceptable.

Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees simultaneously, keeping the bar close to your body and your back flat. Exhale as you stand tall, finishing with your shoulders pulled back and chest up.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower the bar along the same path by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, inhaling as you return to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.
Caution: Maintain a neutral spine throughout. Avoid this exercise if you have any lower back issues without professional guidance.
Variations: Rack Pulls or Plié Dumbbell Squats.

Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees simultaneously, keeping the bar close to your body and your back flat. Exhale as you stand tall, finishing with your shoulders pulled back and chest up.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower the bar along the same path by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, inhaling as you return to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

es: Carga una barra y colócate detrás de ella con una postura amplia y las puntas de los pies ligeramente hacia afuera. Las espinillas deben estar cerca de la barra o tocándola levemente.

Manteniendo la espalda recta y el pecho alto, flexiona caderas y rodillas para descender y agarrar la barra por dentro de las rodillas con un agarre prono a la anchura de los hombros. Las caderas deben quedar más altas que las rodillas. También puedes usar un agarre mixto (una mano en prono y otra en supino) si te resulta más cómodo.

Empuja con los talones y extiende caderas y rodillas a la vez, manteniendo la barra pegada al cuerpo y la espalda recta. Exhala al ponerte de pie, terminando con los hombros hacia atrás y el pecho alto.
Haz una breve pausa arriba y, a continuación, desciende la barra por la misma trayectoria llevando las caderas hacia atrás y flexionando las rodillas. Inhala al volver a la posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Precaución: Mantén la columna neutra durante todo el movimiento. Evita este ejercicio si tienes molestias lumbares sin la supervisión de un profesional.
Variaciones: Rack Pulls o sentadillas plié con mancuerna.

name: Chest Supported Barbell Front Raises
instructions: Place a bar on the ground behind the head of an incline bench.
Lay on the bench face down. With a pronated grip, pick the barbell up from the floor, keeping your arms straight. Allow the bar to hang straight down. This will be your starting position.
To begin, raise the barbell out in front of your head while keeping your arms extended.
Return to the starting position.

en: Place a bar on the ground behind the head of an incline bench.
Lay on the bench face down. With a pronated grip, pick the barbell up from the floor, keeping your arms straight. Allow the bar to hang straight down. This will be your starting position.
To begin, raise the barbell out in front of your head while keeping your arms extended.
Return to the starting position.
es: Coloca a barra on the ground behind the cabeza of an incline banco.
Túmbate on the banco boca abajo. With a pronated grip, pick the barrara up from the suelo, keeping your brazos straight. Allow the barra to hang straight down. Esta será la posición inicial.
To begin, raise the barrara out in front of your cabeza while keeping your brazos extended.
Return to the posición inicial.

name: Stiff Legged Deadlifts (Sumo)
instructions: Place a loaded barbell in front of you and adopt a wide stance. Have your shins gently touching the barbell, lean forward from your hips to take an overhand, narrow grip (index fingers 6-12 inches apart). Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor, a slight bend at your knees and remember to maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your knees stationary, exhale as you drive through your heels to elevate the barbell. You should be pulling through your hamstrings and driving your hips forward at the same time to lift the bar up into a standing position.

After a brief pause in this standing position, lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the barbell close to you as you go. Push your glutes back to help lower the bar towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Inhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variation: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Deadlifts.

es: Coloca a loaded barrara in front of you and adopt a wide stance. Have your shins gently touching the barrara, lean forward from your caderas to take an overmano, agarre cerrado (index fingers 6-12 inches apart). Allow your brazos to hang perpendicular to the suelo, a slight bend at your rodillas and remember to maintain a straight espalda throughout. Esta será la posición inicial.

Whilst keeping your rodillas stationary, espira as you drive through your talones to elevate the barrara. You should be pulling through your isquios and driving your caderas forward at the same time to lift the barra up into a standing posición.

Tras una breve pausa in this standing posición, lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight espalda), keeping the barrara close to you as you go. Push your glúteos espalda to help lower the barra hacia your pies until you feel a stretch in your isquios. Consejo: Remember, this whole movimiento is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the rodilla, but no more) and a straight espalda throughout. Inspira as you perform this part of the movimiento.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Precaución: This is not an exercise for those with espalda issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variación: mancuerna Stiff Legged Deadlifts.

name: Standing Barbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Grab a barbell and adopt a shoulder width, over hand grip. Lift the bar above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar is below your chin.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.

name.es: Press militar con barra (press de hombro)

en: Grab a barbell and adopt a shoulder width, over hand grip. Lift the bar above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar is below your chin.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.
es: Coge a barrara and adopt a hombro width, over mano grip. Lift the barra above your cabeza so that it is directly above your hombros. Mantén your codos locked at this point for safety. Mantén a straight espalda throughout this movimiento. Esta será la posición inicial.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the barra by bending at your codos until the barra is below your chin.
Tras una breve pausa, espira and press the barra espalda into the posición inicial by contracting your deltoid muscles (hombros).
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Precaución: Those with espalda problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variaciones: mancuerna Shoulder Press or multipower Shoulder Press.

name: Smith Machine Calf Raises
instructions: Load the smith machine with the desired amount of weight and place the bar at shoulder height. Step underneath the bar and rotate it so that the weight is resting across the back of your shoulders. So should take hold of the bar loosely just to make sure it does not fall. Take up your stance directly below the bar with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. This is your starting position.
Exhale and contract your calf muscles elevating your heels off the floor bringing the weight onto the balls of your feet. Continue in this fashion until your calves are as tight as they can be.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Seated Calf Raises or Seated Calf Raise Machine.
