es: Load the smith máquina with the desired amount of peso and place the barra at hombro height. Step underneath the barra and rotate it so that the peso is resting across the espalda of your hombros. So should take hold of the barra loosely just to make sure it does not fall. Take up your stance directly below the barra with a hombro width stance and a straight espalda. Esta es la posición inicial.
Espira and contract your calf muscles elevating your talones off the suelo bringing the peso onto the balls of your pies. Continue in this fashion until your gemelos are as tight as they can be.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return the barra along the same path espalda into the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: mancuerna Seated Calf Raises or Seated Calf Raise Machine.

name: Squat Jerk
instructions: Standing with the weight racked on the front of the shoulders, begin with the dip. With your feet directly under your hips, flex the knees without moving the hips backward. Go down only slightly, and reverse direction as powerfully as possible. Drive through the heels create as much speed and force as possible, and be sure to move your head out of the way as the bar leaves the shoulders.

At this moment as the feet leave the floor, the feet must be placed into the receiving position as quickly as possible. In the brief moment, the feet are not actively driving against the platform, the athlete’s effort to push the bar up will drive them down. The feet should move forcefully to just outside the hips, turned out as necessary. Receive the bar with your body in a full squat and the arms fully extended overhead.
Keeping the bar aligned over the front of the heels, your head and chest up, drive throught heels of the feet to move to a standing position. Carefully return the weight to floor.

es: Colócate de pieing with the peso racked on the front of the hombros, begin with the dip. With your pies directly under your caderas, flex the rodillas without moving the caderas espaldaward. Go down only slightly, and reverse direction as powerfully as possible. Drive through the talones create as much speed and force as possible, and be sure to move your cabeza out of the way as the barra leaves the hombros.

At this moment as the pies leave the suelo, the pies must be placed into the receiving posición as quickly as possible. In the brief moment, the pies are not actively driving against the platform, the athlete’s effort to push the barra up will drive them down. The pies should move forcefully to just outside the caderas, turned out as necessary. Receive the barra with your body in a full squat and the brazos fully extended overcabeza.
Manténing the barra aligned over the front of the talones, your cabeza and pecho up, drive throught talones of the pies to move to a standing posición. Carefully return the peso to suelo.

name: Split Snatch
instructions: Begin with a loaded barbell on the floor. The bar should be close to or touching the shins, and a wide grip should be taken on the bar. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar. This will be the starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the front of the heels, raising the bar from the ground. The back angle should stay the same until the bar passes the knees.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, forcefully pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. The feet should move forcefully to a split position, one foot forward one foot back. Receive the bar with your body as low as possible and the arms fully extended overhead.
Keeping the bar aligned over the front of the heels, your head and chest up, drive through heels of the feet to move to a standing position, bringing your feet together.
Carefully return the weight to floor.

en: Begin with a loaded barbell on the floor. The bar should be close to or touching the shins, and a wide grip should be taken on the bar. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar. This will be the starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the front of the heels, raising the bar from the ground. The back angle should stay the same until the bar passes the knees.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

es: Empieza with a loaded barrara on the suelo. The barra should be close to or touching the shins, and a agarre amplio should be taken on the barra. The pies should be directly below the caderas, with the pies turned out as needed. Lower the caderas, with the pecho up and the cabeza looking forward. The hombros should be just in front of the barra. This will be the posición inicial.
Empieza the first pull by driving through the front of the talones, raising the barra from the ground. The espalda angle should stay the same until the barra passes the rodillas.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the caderas rodillas and ankles, driving the barra up as quickly as possible. The barra should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the hombros and allow the codos to flex to the side.

As you move your pies into the receiving posición, forcefully pull yourself below the barra as you elevate the barra overcabeza. The pies should move forcefully to a split posición, one pie forward one pie espalda. Receive the barra with your body as low as possible and the brazos fully extended overcabeza.
Manténing the barra aligned over the front of the talones, your cabeza and pecho up, drive through talones of the pies to move to a standing posición, bringing your pies together.
Carefully return the peso to suelo.

name: Split Jerk
instructions: Standing with the weight racked on the front of the shoulders, begin with the dip. With your feet directly under your hips, flex the knees without moving the hips backward.

Go down only slightly, and reverse direction as powerfully as possible. Drive through the heels create as much speed and force as possible, and be sure to move your head out of the way as the bar leaves the shoulders. At this moment as the feet leave the floor, the feet must be placed into the receiving position as quickly as possible.

In the brief moment the feet are not actively driving against the platform, the athlete’s effort to push the bar up will drive them down. The feet should be moved to a split stance, one foot forward, one foot back, with the knees partially bent. Receive the bar with the arms locked out overhead.
Return to a standing position, bringing the feet together.

name: Split Clean
instructions: With a barbell on the floor close to the shins, take an overhand grip just outside the legs. Lower your hips with the weight focused on the heels, back  straight, head facing forward, chest up, with your shoulders just in front of the bar. This will be your starting position.

Begin the first pull by driving through the heels, extending your knees. Your back angle should stay the same, and your arms should remain straight. Move the weight with control as you continue to above the knees.

Next comes the second pull, the main source of acceleration for the clean. As the bar approaches the mid-thigh position, begin extending through the hips. In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, using speed to move the bar upward. There should be no need to actively pull through the arms to accelerate the weight; at the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly back, with the arms still extended.

As full extension is achieved, transition into the third pull by aggressively shrugging and flexing the arms with the elbows up and out. At peak extension, aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your elbows under the bar as you do so.

Receive the bar with the feet split, aggressively moving one foot forward and one foot back. The bar should be racked onto the protracted shoulders, lightly touching the throat with the hands relaxed. Continue to descend to the bottom position, which will help in the recovery.
Immediately recover by driving through the heels, keeping the torso upright and elbows up. Bring the feet together as you stand up.

en: With a barbell on the floor close to the shins, take an overhand grip just outside the legs. Lower your hips with the weight focused on the heels, back  straight, head facing forward, chest up, with your shoulders just in front of the bar. This will be your starting position.

Begin the first pull by driving through the heels, extending your knees. Your back angle should stay the same, and your arms should remain straight. Move the weight with control as you continue to above the knees.

Next comes the second pull, the main source of acceleration for the clean. As the bar approaches the mid-thigh position, begin extending through the hips. In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, using speed to move the bar upward. There should be no need to actively pull through the arms to accelerate the weight; at the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly back, with the arms still extended.

As full extension is achieved, transition into the third pull by aggressively shrugging and flexing the arms with the elbows up and out. At peak extension, aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your elbows under the bar as you do so.

Receive the bar with the feet split, aggressively moving one foot forward and one foot back. The bar should be racked onto the protracted shoulders, lightly touching the throat with the hands relaxed. Continue to descend to the bottom position, which will help in the recovery.
Immediately recover by driving through the heels, keeping the torso upright and elbows up. Bring the feet together as you stand up.
es: With a barrara on the suelo close to the shins, take an overmano grip just outside the legs. Lower your caderas with the peso focused on the talones, espalda  straight, cabeza facing forward, pecho up, with your hombros just in front of the barra. Esta será la posición inicial.

Empieza the first pull by driving through the talones, extending your rodillas. Your espalda angle should stay the same, and your brazos should remain straight. Move the peso with control as you continue to above the rodillas.

Next comes the second pull, the main source of acceleration for the clean. As the barra approaches the mid-thigh posición, begin extending through the caderas. In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the caderas, rodillas, and ankles, using speed to move the barra upward. There should be no need to actively pull through the brazos to accelerate the peso; at the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly espalda, with the brazos still extended.

As full extension is achieved, transition into the third pull by aggressively shrugging and flexing the brazos with the codos up and out. At peak extension, aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your codos under the barra as you do so.

Receive the barra with the pies split, aggressively moving one pie forward and one pie espalda. The barra should be racked onto the protracted hombros, lightly touching the throat with the manos relaxed. Continue to descend to the bottom posición, which will help in the recovery.
Immediately recover by driving through the talones, keeping the torso upright and codos up. Bring the pies together as you stand up.

name: Snatch Shrug
instructions: Begin with a wide grip, with the bar hanging at the mid thigh position. You can use a hook or overhand grip. Your back should be straight and inclined slightly forward.
Shrug your shoulders towards your ears. While this exercise can usually by loaded with heavier weight than a snatch, avoid overloading to the point that the execution slows down.

en: Begin with a wide grip, with the bar hanging at the mid thigh position. You can use a hook or overhand grip. Your back should be straight and inclined slightly forward.
Shrug your shoulders towards your ears. While this exercise can usually by loaded with heavier weight than a snatch, avoid overloading to the point that the execution slows down.
es: Empieza with a agarre amplio, with the barra hanging at the mid thigh posición. You can use a hook or overmano grip. Your espalda should be straight and inclined slightly forward.
Shrug your hombros hacia your ears. Mientras this exercise can usually by loaded with heavier peso than a snatch, avoid overloading to the point that the execution slows down.

name: Snatch High Pull
instructions: With a barbell on the floor close to the shins, take a wide snatch grip. Lower your hips with the weight focused on the heels, back straight, head facing forward, chest up, with your shoulders just in front of the bar. This will be your starting position.

Begin the first pull by driving through the heels, extending your knees. Your back angle should stay the same, and your arms should remain straight. Move the weight with control as you continue to above the knees.

Next comes the second pull, the main source of acceleration for the pull. As the bar approaches the mid-thigh position, begin extending through the hips. In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, using speed to move the bar upward.

There should be no need to actively pull through the arms to accelerate the weight; at the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly back. Full extension should be violent and abrupt, and ensure that you do not prolong the extension for longer than necessary.

en: With a barbell on the floor close to the shins, take a wide snatch grip. Lower your hips with the weight focused on the heels, back straight, head facing forward, chest up, with your shoulders just in front of the bar. This will be your starting position.

es: With a barrara on the suelo close to the shins, take a wide snatch grip. Lower your caderas with the peso focused on the talones, espalda straight, cabeza facing forward, pecho up, with your hombros just in front of the barra. Esta será la posición inicial.

Empieza the first pull by driving through the talones, extending your rodillas. Your espalda angle should stay the same, and your brazos should remain straight. Move the peso with control as you continue to above the rodillas.

Next comes the second pull, the main source of acceleration for the pull. As the barra approaches the mid-thigh posición, begin extending through the caderas. In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the caderas, rodillas, and ankles, using speed to move the barra upward.

There should be no need to actively pull through the brazos to accelerate the peso; at the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly espalda. Full extension should be violent and abrupt, and ensure that you do not prolong the extension for longer than necessary.

name: Snatch from Boxes
instructions: Begin with a loaded barbell on boxes or stands of the desired height. A wide grip should be taken on the bar. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar, with the elbows pointed out. This will be the starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the front of the heels, raising the bar from the boxes.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, forcefully pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. The feet should move to just outside the hips, turned out as necessary. Receive the bar with your body as low as possible and the arms fully extended overhead.
Keeping the bar aligned over the front of the heels, your head and chest up, drive through heels of the feet to move to a standing position. Carefully return the weight to the boxes.

en: Begin with a loaded barbell on boxes or stands of the desired height. A wide grip should be taken on the bar. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar, with the elbows pointed out. This will be the starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the front of the heels, raising the bar from the boxes.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, forcefully pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. The feet should move to just outside the hips, turned out as necessary. Receive the bar with your body as low as possible and the arms fully extended overhead.
Keeping the bar aligned over the front of the heels, your head and chest up, drive through heels of the feet to move to a standing position. Carefully return the weight to the boxes.
es: Empieza with a loaded barrara on cajónes or stands of the desired height. A agarre amplio should be taken on the barra. The pies should be directly below the caderas, with the pies turned out as needed. Lower the caderas, with the pecho up and the cabeza looking forward. The hombros should be just in front of the barra, with the codos pointed out. This will be the posición inicial.
Empieza the first pull by driving through the front of the talones, raising the barra from the cajónes.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the caderas rodillas and ankles, driving the barra up as quickly as possible. The barra should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the hombros and allow the codos to flex to the side.

As you move your pies into the receiving posición, forcefully pull yourself below the barra as you elevate the barra overcabeza. The pies should move to just outside the caderas, turned out as necessary. Receive the barra with your body as low as possible and the brazos fully extended overcabeza.
Manténing the barra aligned over the front of the talones, your cabeza and pecho up, drive through talones of the pies to move to a standing posición. Carefully return the peso to the cajónes.

name: Snatch Balance
instructions: Begin with the feet in the pulling position, the bar racked across the back of the shoulders, and the hands placed in a wide snatch grip.
Pop the bar with an abrupt dip and drive of the knees, and aggressively drive under the bar, transitioning the feet into the receiving position.
Receive the bar locked out overhead near the bottom of the squat. The torso should remain vertical, lowering the hips between the legs.
Continue to descend to full depth, and return to a standing position. Carefully lower the weight.

en: Begin with the feet in the pulling position, the bar racked across the back of the shoulders, and the hands placed in a wide snatch grip.
Pop the bar with an abrupt dip and drive of the knees, and aggressively drive under the bar, transitioning the feet into the receiving position.
Receive the bar locked out overhead near the bottom of the squat. The torso should remain vertical, lowering the hips between the legs.
Continue to descend to full depth, and return to a standing position. Carefully lower the weight.
es: Empieza with the pies in the pulling posición, the barra racked across the espalda of the hombros, and the manos placed in a wide snatch grip.
Pop the barra with an abrupt dip and drive of the rodillas, and aggressively drive under the barra, transitioning the pies into the receiving posición.
Receive the barra locked out overcabeza near the bottom of the squat. The torso should remain vertical, lowering the caderas between the legs.
Continue to descend to full depth, and return to a standing posición. Carefully lower the peso.

name: Snatch-Grip Deadlift
instructions: The snatch deadlift strengthens the first pull of the snatch. Begin with a wide snatch grip with the barbell placed on the platform. The feet should be directly under the hips, with the feet turned out. Squat down to the bar, keeping the back in absolute extension with the head facing forward.
Initiate the movement by driving through the heels, raising the hips. The back angle should remain the same until the bar passes the knees.
At that point, drive your hips through the bar as you lay back. Return the bar to the platform by reversing the motion.

en: The snatch deadlift strengthens the first pull of the snatch. Begin with a wide snatch grip with the barbell placed on the platform. The feet should be directly under the hips, with the feet turned out. Squat down to the bar, keeping the back in absolute extension with the head facing forward.
Initiate the movement by driving through the heels, raising the hips. The back angle should remain the same until the bar passes the knees.
At that point, drive your hips through the bar as you lay back. Return the bar to the platform by reversing the motion.
es: The snatch deadlift strengthens the first pull of the snatch. Empieza with a wide snatch grip with the barrara placed on the platform. The pies should be directly under the caderas, with the pies turned out. Squat down to the barra, keeping the espalda in absolute extension with the cabeza facing forward.
Initiate the movimiento by driving through the talones, raising the caderas. The espalda angle should remain the same until the barra passes the rodillas.
At that point, drive your caderas through the barra as you lay espalda. Return the barra to the platform by reversing the motion.

name: Skull Crushers (Behind The Head)
instructions: Grab a barbell and lay flat on a bench with the bar pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Adopt a pronated (overhand) grip with your elbows tucked in. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the barbell down towards the back of your head. Be sure to keep your elbows tucked in and stretch your triceps as you go. When you get to within an inch of the bench pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar along the same path to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Skull Crusher or Tricep Rope Extensions.

en: Grab a barbell and lay flat on a bench with the bar pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Adopt a pronated (overhand) grip with your elbows tucked in. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the barbell down towards the back of your head. Be sure to keep your elbows tucked in and stretch your triceps as you go. When you get to within an inch of the bench pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar along the same path to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Skull Crusher or Tricep Rope Extensions.
es: Coge a barrara and lay flat on a banco with the barra pressed straight up hacia the techo. Adopt a pronated (overmano) grip with your codos tucked in. Esta será la posición inicial.

Inspira as you descend the barrara down hacia the espalda of your cabeza. Be sure to keep your codos tucked in and stretch your tríceps as you go. When you get to within an inch of the banco pause for a second.
Then mientras exhaling, use your tríceps to return the barra along the same path to the posición inicial. Your brazos should be fully extended once again and pointing hacia the techo.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: mancuerna Skull Crusher or Tricep Rope Extensions.

name: Palms Down Barbell Wrist Curls Over A Bench
instructions: Grab a barbell and kneel down next to a bench. Lean over the bench so that your forearms are supported with your wrists over hanging (barbell in hand). Adopt an overhand 'palms facing down' grip. This will be your starting position. Tip: Only your wrists should move when performing this exercise.
Exhale and curl the back of your hands up towards your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Palms Down Wrist Curls (Dumbbell)

name.en: Palms Down Barbell Wrist Curls Over A Bench
name.es: Palms Down barra Wrist curl Over A Bench

en: Grab a barbell and kneel down next to a bench. Lean over the bench so that your forearms are supported with your wrists over hanging (barbell in hand). Adopt an overhand 'palms facing down' grip. This will be your starting position. Tip: Only your wrists should move when performing this exercise.
Exhale and curl the back of your hands up towards your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Palms Down Wrist Curls (Dumbbell)
es: Coge a barrara and rodillal down next to a banco. Lean over the banco so that your forebrazos are supported with your wrists over hanging (barrara in mano). Adopt an overmano 'palms facing down' grip. Esta será la posición inicial. Consejo: Only your wrists should move when performing this exercise.
Espira and curl the espalda of your manos up hacia your forebrazos.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and return your manos along the same path to the posición inicial.
Repite el movimiento el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variación: Palms Down Wrist Curls (mancuerna)

aliases: Palms Down Barbell Wrist Curls Over A Bench

name: Seated Good Mornings
instructions: Set up a box in a power rack. The pins should be set at an appropriate height. Begin by stepping under the bar and placing it across the back of the shoulders, not on top of your traps. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and rotate your elbows forward, attempting to bend the bar across your shoulders.

Remove the bar from the rack, creating a tight arch in your lower back. Keep your head facing forward. With your back, shoulders, and core tight, push your knees and butt out and you begin your descent. Sit back with your hips until you are seated on the box. This will be your starting position.
Keeping the bar tight, bend forward at the hips as much as possible. If you set the pins to what would be parallel, you not only have a safety if you fail, but know when to stop.
Pause just above the pins and reverse the motion until your torso it upright.

en: Set up a box in a power rack. The pins should be set at an appropriate height. Begin by stepping under the bar and placing it across the back of the shoulders, not on top of your traps. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and rotate your elbows forward, attempting to bend the bar across your shoulders.

Remove the bar from the rack, creating a tight arch in your lower back. Keep your head facing forward. With your back, shoulders, and core tight, push your knees and butt out and you begin your descent. Sit back with your hips until you are seated on the box. This will be your starting position.
Keeping the bar tight, bend forward at the hips as much as possible. If you set the pins to what would be parallel, you not only have a safety if you fail, but know when to stop.
Pause just above the pins and reverse the motion until your torso it upright.
es: Coloca up a cajón in a rack de potencia. The pins should be set at an appropriate height. Empieza by stepping under the barra and placing it across the espalda of the hombros, not on top of your traps. Squeeze your hombro blades together and rotate your codos forward, attempting to bend the barra across your hombros.

Remove the barra from the rack, creating a tight arch in your zona lumbar. Mantén your cabeza facing forward. With your espalda, hombros, and core tight, push your rodillas and butt out and you begin your descent. Sit espalda with your caderas until you are seated on the cajón. Esta será la posición inicial.
Manténing the barra tight, bend forward at the caderas as much as possible. If you set the pins to what would be parallel, you not only have a safety if you fail, but know when to stop.
Pause just above the pins and reverse the motion until your torso it upright.

name: Seated Barbell Twists
instructions: Grab a barbell and place it on the reverse of your shoulders (just below your neck) and take a seat on a flat bench. This is your starting position.

Exhale and twist your torso clockwise through your core muscles in a controlled fashion until you cannot rotate anymore. Once you have reached this position, inhale and return your torso along the same path back to the starting position.

Exhale once more and rotate your torso anti-clockwise through your core muscles in a controlled fashion until you cannot rotate anymore. Once you have reached this position, inhale and return your torso along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: V-Sit Twist (Medicine Ball) or Decline Oblique Crunches.

en: Grab a barbell and place it on the reverse of your shoulders (just below your neck) and take a seat on a flat bench. This is your starting position.

Exhale and twist your torso clockwise through your core muscles in a controlled fashion until you cannot rotate anymore. Once you have reached this position, inhale and return your torso along the same path back to the starting position.

Exhale once more and rotate your torso anti-clockwise through your core muscles in a controlled fashion until you cannot rotate anymore. Once you have reached this position, inhale and return your torso along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: V-Sit Twist (Medicine Ball) or Decline Oblique Crunches.
es: Coge a barrara and place it on the reverse of your hombros (just below your neck) and take a seat on a flat banco. Esta es la posición inicial.

Espira and twist your torso clockwise through your core muscles in a controlled fashion until you cannot rotate anymore. Once you have reached this posición, inspira and return your torso along the same path espalda to the posición inicial.

Espira once more and rotate your torso anti-clockwise through your core muscles in a controlled fashion until you cannot rotate anymore. Once you have reached this posición, inspira and return your torso along the same path espalda to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: V-Sit Twist (Medicine Ball) or Decline Oblique Crunches.

name: Stiff Legged Deadlift From A Deficit
instructions: Begin standing while holding a bar at arm’s length in front of you.  You can stand on a raised platform to increase the range of motion.

Begin by flexing the knees slightly, and then flex at the hip, moving your butt back as far as possible, lowering the torso as far as flexibility allows. The back should remain in absolute extension at all times, and the bar should remain in contact with the legs. If done properly, there should be heavy tension felt in the hamstrings.
Reverse the motion to return to the starting position.

name.en: Stiff Legged Deadlift From A Deficit
name.es: Piernas rígidas peso muerto From A Deficit

en: Begin standing while holding a bar at arm’s length in front of you.  You can stand on a raised platform to increase the range of motion.

Begin by flexing the knees slightly, and then flex at the hip, moving your butt back as far as possible, lowering the torso as far as flexibility allows. The back should remain in absolute extension at all times, and the bar should remain in contact with the legs. If done properly, there should be heavy tension felt in the hamstrings.
Reverse the motion to return to the starting position.
es: Empieza standing while holding a barra at brazo’s length in front of you.  You can stand on a raised platform to increase the range of motion.

Empieza by flexing the rodillas slightly, and then flex at the cadera, moving your butt espalda as far as possible, lowering the torso as far as flexibility allows. The espalda should remain in absolute extension at all times, and the barra should remain in contact with the legs. If done properly, there should be heavy tension felt in the isquios.
Reverse the motion to return to the posición inicial.

aliases: Stiff Legged Deadlift From A Deficit

name: Reverse Triceps Bench Press
instructions: Lie back on a flat bench.  Using a close, supinated grip (around shoulder width), lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with your arms locked extended in front of you and perpendicular to the floor.  This will be your starting position.

As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the bar on your middle chest. Tip: Make sure that as opposed to a regular bench press, you keep the elbows close to the torso at all times in order to maximize triceps involvement.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your triceps muscles.  Lock your arms in the contracted position, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again. Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.

Caution: If you are new at this exercise, it is advised that you use a spotter.  If no spotter is available, then be conservative with the amount of weight used.  Also, beware of letting the bar drift too far forward.  You want the bar to fall around the area between the sternum and upper abs.
Variation: This exercise can also be performed with an e-z bar using the inner handle as well as dumbbells.

en: Lie back on a flat bench.  Using a close, supinated grip (around shoulder width), lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with your arms locked extended in front of you and perpendicular to the floor.  This will be your starting position.

As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the bar on your middle chest. Tip: Make sure that as opposed to a regular bench press, you keep the elbows close to the torso at all times in order to maximize triceps involvement.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your triceps muscles.  Lock your arms in the contracted position, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again. Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.

Variation: This exercise can also be performed with an e-z bar using the inner handle as well as dumbbells.
es: Túmbate espalda on a flat banco.  Using a close, supinated grip (around hombro width), lift the barra from the rack and hold it straight over you with your brazos locked extended in front of you and perpendicular to the suelo.  Esta será la posición inicial.

As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the barra on your middle pecho. Consejo: Make sure that as opposed to a regular banco press, you keep the codos close to the torso at all times in order to maximize tríceps involvement.

After a second pause, bring the barra espalda to the posición inicial as you breathe out and push the barra using your tríceps muscles.  Lock your brazos in the contracted posición, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again. Consejo: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movimiento for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the barra espalda in the rack.

Precaución: If you are new at this exercise, it is advised that you use a spotter.  If no spotter is available, then be conservative with the amount of peso used.  Also, beware of letting the barra drift too far forward.  You want the barra to fall around the area between the sternum and upper abs.
Variación: This exercise can also be performed with an e-z barra using the inner asa as well as mancuernas.

name: Scapular Pull Ups
instructions: Take hold of a pull up bar with a wide, overhand grip. Lift your feet up and off the floor so your body is now suspended. This is your starting position.
Exhale and pull your shoulder blades together, pushing your chest upward. Use your arms slightly to help aid in this process.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower your body back on the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Shrug or Smith Machine Shrug.

en: Take hold of a pull up bar with a wide, overhand grip. Lift your feet up and off the floor so your body is now suspended. This is your starting position.
Exhale and pull your shoulder blades together, pushing your chest upward. Use your arms slightly to help aid in this process.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower your body back on the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Shrug or Smith Machine Shrug.
es: Take hold of a pull up barra with a wide, overmano grip. Lift your pies up and off the suelo so your body is now suspended. Esta es la posición inicial.
Espira and pull your hombro blades together, pushing your pecho upward. Use your brazos slightly to help aid in this process.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and slowly lower your body espalda on the same path into its posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: barra Shrug or multipower Shrug.

name: Reverse Grip Barbell Preacher Curls
instructions: Grab a barbell with an overhand grip and take a seat on a preacher curl machine. Place your upper arms over the pad of the machine with your palms facing downward. Set the height of the seat so that your arm pits are snug on top of the pad and have your arms extended. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and contract your biceps to lift the barbell up towards your shoulders. Keep your triceps on the pad and squeeze your biceps until they are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the barbell along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Reverse Barbell Curl or Reverse Plate Curls.

en: Grab a barbell with an overhand grip and take a seat on a preacher curl machine. Place your upper arms over the pad of the machine with your palms facing downward. Set the height of the seat so that your arm pits are snug on top of the pad and have your arms extended. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and contract your biceps to lift the barbell up towards your shoulders. Keep your triceps on the pad and squeeze your biceps until they are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the barbell along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Reverse Barbell Curl or Reverse Plate Curls.
es: Coge a barrara with an overmano grip and take a seat on a preacher curl máquina. Coloca your upper brazos over the pad of the máquina with your palms facing downward. Coloca the height of the seat so that your brazo pits are snug on top of the pad and have your brazos extended. Esta será la posición inicial.
Espira and contract your bíceps to lift the barrara up hacia your hombros. Mantén your tríceps on the pad and squeeze your bíceps until they are fully contracted and the barrara is at hombro height.
Tras una breve pausa, inspira and slowly lower the barrara along the same path espalda into its posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Reverse barra Curl or Reverse Plate Curls.

name: Reverse Grip Barbell Curls
instructions: Grab a barbell with a shoulder width grip (palms facing your thighs), and allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor. Adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the barbell upward toward your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so and remember, only your forearms should move.
Continue in this fashion until your biceps are fully flexed and the barbell is at shoulder height.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the barbell along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Reverse Plate Curls or Reverse Cable Curls.

en: Grab a barbell with a shoulder width grip (palms facing your thighs), and allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor. Adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the barbell upward toward your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so and remember, only your forearms should move.
Continue in this fashion until your biceps are fully flexed and the barbell is at shoulder height.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the barbell along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Reverse Plate Curls or Reverse Cable Curls.
es: Coge a barrara with a hombro width grip (palms facing your thighs), and allow your brazos the hang perpendicular to the suelo. Adopt a standing posición with a hombro width stance and a straight espalda. Mantén your codos pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. Esta será la posición inicial.
Whilst keeping your upper brazos stationary, curl the barrara upward toward your hombros by flexing your bíceps. Espira as you do so and remember, only your forebrazos should move.
Continue in this fashion until your bíceps are fully flexed and the barrara is at hombro height.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the barrara along the same path toward the posición inicial as you inspira.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Reverse Plate Curls or Reverse Polea Curls.

name: Reverse Grip Bent Over Barbell Rows
instructions: Grab a barbell (underhand grip, palms facing away from you) and adopt a shoulder width stance whilst leaning forward from your hips. Maintain a straight back as you do so, making sure to flex your knees as you lean. Lean forward until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor whilst allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to it. This is your starting position.

Whilst holding a stationary torso and exhaling, pull the barbell up towards your belly button by attempting to squeeze your elbows together behind your back. In order to make this movement possible, contract the muscles in your back by pulling your shoulder blades together. At the top contracted position, pause for a second before returning the barbell along the same path to the starting position (inhale as you do so).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues.
Maintain perfect posture throughout to minimise possibility of injury.
Variations: Dumbbell Bent Over Row or Kettlebell Bent Over Row.

name.en: Reverse Grip Bent Over Barbell Rows
name.es: Agarre inverso Bent Over barra remos

en: Grab a barbell (underhand grip, palms facing away from you) and adopt a shoulder width stance whilst leaning forward from your hips. Maintain a straight back as you do so, making sure to flex your knees as you lean. Lean forward until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor whilst allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to it. This is your starting position.

Whilst holding a stationary torso and exhaling, pull the barbell up towards your belly button by attempting to squeeze your elbows together behind your back. In order to make this movement possible, contract the muscles in your back by pulling your shoulder blades together. At the top contracted position, pause for a second before returning the barbell along the same path to the starting position (inhale as you do so).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues.

Variations: Dumbbell Bent Over Row or Kettlebell Bent Over Row.
es: Coge a barrara (undermano grip, palms facing away from you) and adopt a hombro width stance mientras leaning forward from your caderas. Mantén a straight espalda as you do so, making sure to flex your rodillas as you lean. Lean forward until your torso is nearly parallel to the suelo mientras allowing your brazos to hang perpendicular to it. Esta es la posición inicial.

Whilst holding a stationary torso and exhaling, pull the barrara up hacia your belly button by attempting to squeeze your codos together behind your espalda. In order to make this movimiento possible, contract the muscles in your espalda by pulling your hombro blades together. At the top contracted posición, pause for a second before returning the barrara along the same path to the posición inicial (inspira as you do so).
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Precaución:
This exercise is not recommended for people with espalda issues.
Mantén perfect posture throughout to minimise possibility of injury.
Variaciones: mancuerna Bent Over Row or Kettlebell Bent Over Row.

aliases: Reverse Grip Bent Over Barbell Rows

name: Barbell Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Place a barbell on the back of your shoulders and position yourself so that one foot is flat on the floor and the other is behind you, at knee height supported by a bench (an exaggerated step's distance away). This will be your starting position.

From here, start to descend by bending at your knees (maintaining good posture) until you create a 90 degree angle at your front knee. As you descend you should be inhaling and ensuring that your front knee does not extend past your toes.
Pause for a brief moment at the bottom of the movement, before exhaling and driving up through your front heel and thigh, until you return to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Split Squats or TRX Split Squats.

en: Place a barbell on the back of your shoulders and position yourself so that one foot is flat on the floor and the other is behind you, at knee height supported by a bench (an exaggerated step's distance away). This will be your starting position.

From here, start to descend by bending at your knees (maintaining good posture) until you create a 90 degree angle at your front knee. As you descend you should be inhaling and ensuring that your front knee does not extend past your toes.
Pause for a brief moment at the bottom of the movement, before exhaling and driving up through your front heel and thigh, until you return to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Split Squats or TRX Split Squats.
es: Coloca a barrara on the espalda of your hombros and posición yourself so that one pie is flat on the suelo and the other is behind you, at rodilla height supported by a banco (an exaggerated step's distance away). Esta será la posición inicial.

From here, start to descend by bending at your rodillas (maintaining good posture) until you create a 90 degree angle at your front rodilla. As you descend you should be inhaling and ensuring that your front rodilla does not extend past your toes.
Pause for a brief moment at the bottom of the movimiento, before exhaling and driving up through your front talón and thigh, until you return to your posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: mancuerna Split Squats or TRX Split Squats.

name: Rack Pulls
instructions: Place a barbell on the safety bars within a squat rack. Make sure that the bars are set up at the desired height (below/above your knees or mid thigh) and adopt a deadlift position. So step up to the bar with your feet placed underneath your hips, a slight bend at your knees, hips pushed back, a slight arch in your lower back as you lean forward to grab the barbell with an overhand, shoulder width grip. This will be your starting position.

Keeping your head up, drive force through your heels and hips to lift the barbell. Exhale as you do so and use the muscles in your back to help achieve an upright standing position. Tip: Keep the bar close to your body and maintain a straight back throughout the movement. Once you are in this position, pull your shoulder blades together and push your chest out.
Next, inhale and reverse the movement, returning the barbell along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Deadlifts

es: Coloca a barrara on the safety barras within a squat rack. Make sure that the barras are set up at the desired height (below/above your rodillas or mid thigh) and adopt a deadlift posición. So step up to the barra with your pies placed underneath your caderas, a slight bend at your rodillas, caderas pushed espalda, a slight arch in your zona lumbar as you lean forward to grab the barrara with an overmano, hombro width grip. Esta será la posición inicial.

Manténing your cabeza up, drive force through your talones and caderas to lift the barrara. Espira as you do so and use the muscles in your espalda to help achieve an upright standing posición. Consejo: Mantén the barra close to your body and maintain a straight espalda throughout the movimiento. Once you are in this posición, pull your hombro blades together and push your pecho out.
Next, inspira and reverse the movimiento, returning the barrara along the same path espalda into the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variación: barra Deadlifts

name: Barbell Shoulder Press (Behind The Head)
instructions: Take a seat on a flat bench (no back rest) with a barbell in the rack above you. Adopt a wider than shoulder width grip and lift the bar off the rack so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar (behind your head) by bending at your elbows your elbows are just past 90 degrees.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
Caution: You should always have a spotter when attempting this movement. If a spotter is not available you should be conservative with the weight you are using.
Variation: Smith Machine Shoulder Press.

Whilst inhaling, start to lower the barra (behind your cabeza) by bending at your codos your codos are just past 90 degrees.
Tras una breve pausa, espira and press the barra espalda into the posición inicial by contracting your deltoid muscles (hombros).

name: Push Jerk
instructions: Standing with the weight racked on the front of the shoulders, begin with the dip. With your feet directly under your hips, flex the knees without moving the hips backward. Go down only slightly, and reverse direction as powerfully as possible.
Drive through the heels create as much speed and force as possible, and be sure to move your head out of the way as the bar leaves the shoulders.

At this moment as the feet leave the floor, the feet must be placed into the receiving position as quickly as possible. In the brief moment the feet are not actively driving against the platform, the athletes effort to push the bar up will drive them down. The feet should be moved to a slightly wider stance, with the knees partially bent.
Receive the bar with the arms locked out overhead.
Return to a standing position.

Drive through the talones create as much speed and force as possible, and be sure to move your cabeza out of the way as the barra leaves the hombros.

At this moment as the pies leave the suelo, the pies must be placed into the receiving posición as quickly as possible. In the brief moment the pies are not actively driving against the platform, the athletes effort to push the barra up will drive them down. The pies should be moved to a slightly wider stance, with the rodillas partially bent.
Receive the barra with the brazos locked out overcabeza.
Return to a standing posición.

name: Barbell Press Sit Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle with your feet planted on the floor. Take hold of a barbell and keep it close to your chest. Tip: For this movement you may either need a partner to hold your feet down or gently place additional weights on your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst pressing the barbell directly towards the ceiling at the same time. Continue to sit up as you push the weight as high towards the ceiling as possible.
Once your arms are vertical and your abdominal muscles are as tight as they can be, pause briefly, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Otis-Up or Crunches.

en: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle with your feet planted on the floor. Take hold of a barbell and keep it close to your chest. Tip: For this movement you may either need a partner to hold your feet down or gently place additional weights on your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst pressing the barbell directly towards the ceiling at the same time. Continue to sit up as you push the weight as high towards the ceiling as possible.
Once your arms are vertical and your abdominal muscles are as tight as they can be, pause briefly, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Otis-Up or Crunches.
es: Coge a mat and lay down flat on your espalda. Have your rodillas bent to create a 90 degree angle with your pies planted on the suelo. Take hold of a barrara and keep it close to your pecho. Consejo: For this movimiento you may either need a partner to hold your pies down or gently place additional pesos on your pies. Esta será la posición inicial.

To initiate the movimiento, espira and roll your hombros up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles mientras pressing the barrara directly hacia the techo at the same time. Continue to sit up as you push the peso as high hacia the techo as possible.
Once your brazos are vertical and your abdominal muscles are as tight as they can be, pause briefly, inspira and return your body espalda along the same path into the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Otis-Up or Crunches.

name: Narrow Grip EZ Bar Preacher Curls
instructions: Grab an EZ Curl bar, loaded up with the precribed amount of weight and take a seat on a preacher curl machine. Place your upper arms over the pad of the machine with your palms facing your shoulders. Start with your biceps fully contracted and the bar close to your shoulders. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly lower the bar until your upper arms are extended and your biceps are fully stretched.
Exhale and contract your biceps to lift the bar back up to the starting position.
Squeeze your biceps for a second at the contracted position and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Curls or Dumbbell Preacher Curls.

name: Power Snatch from Boxes
instructions: Begin with a loaded barbell on boxes or stands of the desired height. A wide grip should be taken on the bar. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar, with the elbows pointed out. This will be the starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the front of the heels, raising the bar from the boxes.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, forcefully pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. The feet should move to just outside the hips, turned out as necessary. Receive the bar above a full squat and with the arms fully extended overhead.
Keeping the bar aligned over the front of the heels, your head and chest up, drive through heels of the feet to move to a standing position. Carefully return the weight to the boxes.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the caderas rodillas and ankles, driving the barra up as quickly as possible. The barra should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the hombros and allow the codos to flex to the side.

As you move your pies into the receiving posición, forcefully pull yourself below the barra as you elevate the barra overcabeza. The pies should move to just outside the caderas, turned out as necessary. Receive the barra above a full squat and with the brazos fully extended overcabeza.
Manténing the barra aligned over the front of the talones, your cabeza and pecho up, drive through talones of the pies to move to a standing posición. Carefully return the peso to the cajónes.

name: Power Snatch
instructions: Begin with a loaded barbell held at the mid thigh position with a wide grip. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar. This will be the starting position.

Begin the pull by driving through the heels, raising the bar. Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body.
Continue raising the bar to the overhead position, as your arms are fully extended above your head gently bend at the knees to absorb some of the pressure through your lower body.
Return the bar to the floor in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.

name: Power Jerk
instructions: Standing with the weight racked on the front of the shoulders, begin with the dip. With your feet directly under your hips, flex the knees without moving the hips backward. Go down only slightly, and reverse direction as powerfully as possible.
Drive through the heels create as much speed and force as possible, and be sure to move your head out of the way as the bar leaves the shoulders.

At this moment as the feet leave the floor, the feet must be placed into the receiving position as quickly as possible. In the brief moment the feet are not actively driving against the platform, the athletes effort to push the bar up will drive them down. The feet should be moved to a slightly wider stance, with the knees partially bent.
Receive the bar with the arms locked out overhead.
Return to a standing position.

Drive through the talones create as much speed and force as possible, and be sure to move your cabeza out of the way as the barra leaves the hombros.

At this moment as the pies leave the suelo, the pies must be placed into the receiving posición as quickly as possible. Receive the barra with the brazos locked out overcabeza.
Return to a standing posición.

name: Power Clean from Boxes
instructions: With a barbell on boxes of the desired height, take a grip just outside the legs. Lower your hips with the weight focused on the heels, back  straight, head facing forward, chest up, with your shoulders just in front of the bar. This will be your starting position.

Begin the first pull by driving through the heels, extending your knees. Your back angle should stay the same, and your arms should remain straight.  As the bar approaches the mid-thigh position, begin extending through the hips.

In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, using speed to move the bar upward. There should be no need to actively pull through the arms to accelerate the weight. At the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly back, with the arms still extended.

As full extension is achieved, transition into the third pull by aggressively shrugging and flexing the arms with the elbows up and out. At peak extension, pull yourself under the bar far enough that it can be racked onto the shoulders, rotating your elbows under the bar as you do so. The bar should be racked onto the protracted shoulders, lightly touching the throat with the hands relaxed.

Immediately recover by driving through the heels, keeping the torso upright and elbows up. Continue until you have risen to a standing position, and complete the repetition by returning the weight to the boxes.

Empieza the first pull by driving through the talones, extending your rodillas. Your espalda angle should stay the same, and your brazos should remain straight.  As the barra approaches the mid-thigh posición, begin extending through the caderas.

name: Power Clean
instructions: Note: This exercise is extremely complex and requires the execution of many phases.
Phase 1: Starting Position
Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder width apart and toes pointing out slightly.
Squat down and grasp the bar with a closed, pronated grip. Your hands should be slightly wider than shoulder width apart outside knees with elbows fully extended.
Place the bar about 1 inch in front of your shins and over the balls of your feet.
Your back should be flat or slightly arched, your chest held up and out and your shoulder blades should be retracted.
Keep your head in a neutral position (in line with vertebral column and not tilted or rotated) with your eyes focused straight ahead. Inhale during this phase.
Phase 2: First Pull Phase

Lift the bar from the floor by forcefully extending the hips and the knees as you exhale.  Tip: The upper torso should maintain the same angle.  Do not bend at the waist yet and do not let the hips rise before the shoulders (this would have the effect of pushing the glutes in the air and stretching the hamstrings.
Keep elbows fully extended with the head in a neutral position and the shoulders over the bar.
As the bar raises keep it as close to the shins as possible.
Phase 3: Transition or Scoop Phase
As the bar passes the knees, thrust your hips forward and slightly bend the knees to avoid locking them. Tip: At this point your thighs should be against the bar.
Keep the back flat or slightly arched, elbows fully extended and your head neutral. Tip: You will hold your breath until the next phase.
Phase 4: Second Pull Phase
Inhale and then forcefully and quickly extend your hips and knees and stand on your toes.

Keep the bar as close to your body as possible.  Tip: Your back should be flat with the elbows pointed out to the sides and your head in a neutral position.  Also, keep your shoulders over the bar and arms straight as long as possible.
When your lower body joints are fully extended, shrug the shoulders upward rapidly without letting the elbows flex yet. Exhale during this portion of the movement.
As the shoulders reach their highest elevation flex your elbows to begin pulling your body under the bar.

Continue to pull the arms as high and as long as possible. Tip: Due to the explosive nature of this phase, your torso will be erect or with an arched back, your head will be tilted back slightly and your feet may lose contact with the floor.
Phase 5: Catch Phase
After the lower body has fully extended and the bar reaches near maximal height, pull your body under the bar and rotate the arms around and under the bar.
Simultaneously, flex the hips and knees into a quarter squat position.
Once the arms are under the bar, inhale and then lift your elbows to position the upper arms parallel to the floor. Rack the bar across the front of your collar bones and front shoulder muscles.
Catch the bar with an erect and tight torso, a neutral head position and flat feet. Exhale during this movement.
Stand up by extending the hips and knees to a fully erect position.
Phase 6: Downward Movement Phase
Lower the bar by gradually reducing the muscular tension of the arms to allow a controlled descent of the bar to the thighs. Inhale during this movement.
Simultaneously flex the hips and knees to cushion the impact of the bar on the thighs.
Squat down with the elbows fully extended until the bar touches the floor.
Start over at Phase 1 and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

es: Note: This exercise is extremely complex and requires the execution of many phases.
Phase 1: Starting Position
Colócate de pie with your pies slightly wider than hombro width apart and toes pointing out slightly.
Squat down and grasp the barra with a closed, pronated grip. Your manos should be slightly wider than hombro width apart outside rodillas with codos fully extended.
Coloca the barra about 1 inch in front of your shins and over the balls of your pies.
Your espalda should be flat or slightly arched, your pecho held up and out and your hombro blades should be retracted.
Mantén your cabeza in a neutral posición (in line with vertebral column and not tilted or rotated) with your eyes focused straight acabeza. Inspira during this phase.
Phase 2: First Pull Phase

Lift the barra from the suelo by forcefully extending the caderas and the rodillas as you espira.  Consejo: The upper torso should maintain the same angle.  Do not bend at the waist yet and do not let the caderas rise before the hombros (this would have the effect of pushing the glúteos in the air and stretching the isquios.
Mantén codos fully extended with the cabeza in a neutral posición and the hombros over the barra.
As the barra raises keep it as close to the shins as possible.
Phase 3: Transition or Scoop Phase
As the barra passes the rodillas, thrust your caderas forward and slightly bend the rodillas to avoid locking them. Consejo: At this point your thighs should be against the barra.
Mantén the espalda flat or slightly arched, codos fully extended and your cabeza neutral. Consejo: You will hold your breath until the next phase.
Phase 4: Second Pull Phase
Inspira and then forcefully and quickly extend your caderas and rodillas and stand on your toes.

Mantén the barra as close to your body as possible.  Consejo: Your espalda should be flat with the codos pointed out to the sides and your cabeza in a neutral posición.  Also, keep your hombros over the barra and brazos straight as long as possible.
When your lower body joints are fully extended, shrug the hombros upward rapidly without letting the codos flex yet. Espira during this portion of the movimiento.
As the hombros reach their highest elevation flex your codos to begin pulling your body under the barra.

Continue to pull the brazos as high and as long as possible. Consejo: Due to the explosive nature of this phase, your torso will be erect or with an arched espalda, your cabeza will be tilted espalda slightly and your pies may lose contact with the suelo.
Phase 5: Catch Phase
After the lower body has fully extended and the barra reaches near maximal height, pull your body under the barra and rotate the brazos around and under the barra.
Simultaneously, flex the caderas and rodillas into a quarter squat posición.

Once the brazos are under the barra, inspira and then lift your codos to posición the upper brazos parallel to the suelo. Rack the barra across the front of your collar bones and front hombro muscles.
Catch the barra with an erect and tight torso, a neutral cabeza posición and flat pies. Espira during this movimiento.
Colócate de pie up by extending the caderas and rodillas to a fully erect posición.
Phase 6: Downward Movement Phase
Lower the barra by gradually reducing the muscular tension of the brazos to allow a controlled descent of the barra to the thighs. Inspira during this movimiento.
Simultaneously flex the caderas and rodillas to cushion the impact of the barra on the thighs.
Squat down with the codos fully extended until the barra touches the suelo.
Start over at Phase 1 and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Pin Presses
instructions: Pin presses remove the eccentric phase of the bench press, developing starting strength. They also allow you to train a desired range of motion.

The bench should be set up in a power rack. Set the pins to the desired point in your range of motion, whether it just be lockout or an inch off of your chest. The bar should be moved to the pins and prepared for lifting.

Begin by lying on the bench, with the bar directly above the contact point during your regular bench.  Tuck your feet underneath you and arch your back. Using the bar to help support your weight, lift your shoulder off the bench and retract them, squeezing the shoulder blades together. Use your feet to drive your traps into the bench. Maintain this tight body position throughout the movement.
You can take a standard bench grip, or shoulder width to focus on the triceps.  The bar, wrist, and elbow should stay in line at all times. Focus on squeezing the bar and trying to pull it apart.
Drive the bar up with as much force as possible. The elbows should be tucked in until lockout.
Return the bar to the pins, pausing before beginning the next repetition.

The banco should be set up in a rack de potencia. Coloca the pins to the desired point in your range of motion, whether it just be lockout or an inch off of your pecho. The barra should be moved to the pins and prepared for lifting.

Empieza by lying on the banco, with the barra directly above the contact point during your regular banco.  Tuck your pies underneath you and arch your espalda. Using the barra to help support your peso, lift your hombro off the banco and retract them, squeezing the hombro blades together. Use your pies to drive your traps into the banco. Mantén this tight body posición throughout the movimiento.
You can take a standard banco grip, or hombro width to focus on the tríceps.  The barra, wrist, and codo should stay in line at all times. Focus on squeezing the barra and trying to pull it apart.
Drive the barra up with as much force as possible. The codos should be tucked in until lockout.
Return the barra to the pins, pausing before beginning the next repetition.

name: Barbell Overhead squats
instructions: Set the pins of a squat rack at shoulder height. Place your barbell on those pins and load up with the required weight. Now take a wider than shoulder width grip and lift the bar off the rack keeping it close to your body. Next, press the bar above your head and fully extend your arms. Lock out your elbows and take a small step backwards. Adopt a shoulders width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and arms perpendicular to the ground..
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

Lock out your codos and take a small step espaldawards. Adopt a hombros width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Mantén a straight espalda throughout this movimiento. Esta será la posición inicial.

Inspira and begin to descend by bending at your rodillas and caderas. Your peso should be put predominantly through your talones as you sit espalda into the movimiento until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the suelo.

Tras una breve pausa, espira and return the barra to the posición inicial along the same path, push through your talones with the force being driven through your cuádriceps and glúteos to do so. name: Muscle Snatch
instructions: Begin with a loaded barbell held at the mid thigh position with a wide grip. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar. This will be the starting position.

Begin the pull by driving through the heels, raising the bar. Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body.
Continue raising the bar to the overhead position.

As the bar reaches the overhead position, squat underneath it to absorb some of the pressure through your lower body. Once stable, extend your body into an upright position before returning the bar to the floor in a controlled manner.

es: Empieza with a loaded barrara held at the mid thigh posición with a agarre amplio. The pies should be directly below the caderas, with the pies turned out as needed. Lower the caderas, with the pecho up and the cabeza looking forward. The hombros should be just in front of the barra. This will be the posición inicial.

Empieza the pull by driving through the talones, raising the barra. Transition into the second pull by extending through the caderas rodillas and ankles, driving the barra up as quickly as possible. The barra should be close to the body.
Continue raising the barra to the overcabeza posición.

As the barra reaches the overcabeza posición, squat underneath it to absorb some of the pressure through your lower body. Once stable, extend your body into an upright posición before returning the barra to the suelo in a controlled manner.

name: Lying High Bench Barbell Curls
instructions: Lie face forward on a tall flat bench while holding a barbell with a supinated grip (palms facing up). Tip: If you are holding a barbell grab it using a shoulder-width grip and if you are using an E-Z Bar grab it on the inner handles.  Your upper body should be positioned in a way that the upper chest is over the end of the bench and the barbell is hanging in front of you with the arms extended and perpendicular to the floor.  This will be your starting position.
While keeping the elbows in and the upper arms stationary, curl the weight up in a semi-circular motion as you contract the biceps and exhale.  Hold at the top of the movement for a second.

As you inhale, slowly go back to the starting position.  Tip: Maintain full control of the weight at all times and avoid any swinging.  Remember, only the forearms should move throughout the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: If no tall bench is available you can opt for an adjustable bench and you can place it at an incline that allows you to have your arms fully extended without the bar touching the floor.
You can also perform the exercise using dumbbells.

Consejo: If you are holding a barrara grab it using a hombro-width grip and if you are using an E-Z Bar grab it on the inner asas.  Your upper body should be posicióned in a way that the upper pecho is over the end of the banco and the barrara is hanging in front of you with the brazos extended and perpendicular to the suelo.  Esta será la posición inicial.
Mientras keeping the codos in and the upper brazos stationary, curl the peso up in a semi-circular motion as you contract the bíceps and espira.  Hold at the top of the movimiento for a second.

As you inspira, slowly go espalda to the posición inicial.  Consejo: Mantén full control of the peso at all times and avoid any swinging.  Remember, only the forebrazos should move throughout the movimiento.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.

Variaciones: If no tall banco is available you can opt for an adjustable banco and you can place it at an incline that allows you to have your brazos fully extended without the barra touching the suelo.
You can also perform the exercise using mancuernas.

name: Narrow Grip Barbell Tricep Press To Chin
instructions: Lay flat on a bench with a barbell in hand. Take a narrow grip (where if your thumbs were extended, would touch) and press the bar directly above your chin so your arms are at full extension. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by allowing your elbows to flare out wide until the bar is about 1 inch away from your chin.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your triceps.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Caution: You should always have a spotter when attempting this movement. If a spotter is not available you should be conservative with the weight you are using.
Variations: Tricep Extension (Rope) or Incline Dumbbell Kickbacks.

name: Lying T Bar Row
instructions: Place a cambered bar underneath an exercise bench.
Lie face down on the exercise bench and grab the bar using a palms down (pronated grip) that is wider than shoulder width. This will be your starting position.
As you exhale row the bar up as you keep the elbows close to your body to either your chest, in order to target the upper mid back, or to your stomach if targeting the lats is your goal.
After a second hold at the top, lower back down to the starting position slowly as you inhale.

Variations: A regular barbell can be used, but a cambered barbell gives you a greater range of motion. You can also turn this into a rear delt movement by flaring the elbows out instead of keeping them close to your torso.
