As you espira row the barra up as you keep the codos close to your body to either your pecho, in order to target the upper mid espalda, or to your stomach if targeting the dorsales is your goal.
After a second hold at the top, zona lumbar down to the posición inicial slowly as you inspira.

Variaciones: A regular barrara can be used, but a cambered barrara gives you a greater range of motion. You can also turn this into a rear delt movimiento by flaring the codos out instead of keeping them close to your torso.

name: Landmine 180's
instructions: Position a bar into a landmine or securely anchor it in a corner. Load the bar to an appropriate weight.
Raise the bar from the floor, taking it to shoulder height with both hands with your arms extended in front of you. Adopt a wide stance. This will be your starting position.
Perform the movement by rotating the trunk and hips as you swing the weight all the way down to one side. Keep your arms extended throughout the exercise.
Reverse the motion to swing the weight all the way to the opposite side.
Continue alternating the movement until the set is complete.

en: Position a bar into a landmine or securely anchor it in a corner. Load the bar to an appropriate weight.

Reverse the motion to swing the weight all the way to the opposite side.
Continue alternating the movement until the set is complete.
es: Position a barra into a landmine or securely anchor it in a corner. Load the barra to an appropriate peso.
Raise the barra from the suelo, taking it to hombro height with both manos with your brazos extended in front of you. Adopt a wide stance. Esta será la posición inicial.
Perform the movimiento by rotating the trunk and caderas as you swing the peso all the way down to one side. Mantén your brazos extended throughout the exercise.
Reverse the motion to swing the peso all the way to the opposite side.
Continue alternating the movimiento until the set is complete.

name: Narrow Grip Bench Press
instructions: Lay flat on a bench with a barbell in the rack above you. Take a close grip (6-12 inches between your index fingers) and lift the bar off the rack so that it is directly above your chest. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. This will be your starting position.

Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar gently touches the middle of your chest. Your elbows should remain close together and should brush along the sides of your torso as you go.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back along the same path into the starting position by contracting your triceps and pectoral muscles.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
Caution: You should always have a spotter when attempting this movement. If a spotter is not available you should be conservative with the weight you are using.
Variation: Smith Machine Close Grip Bench Press.

name: Jerk Dip Squat
instructions: This movement strengthens the dip portion of the jerk. Begin with the bar racked in the jerk position, with the shoulders forward to create a shelf and the bar lightly contacting the throat. The feet should be directly under the hips, with the feet turned out as is comfortable.

Keeping the torso vertical, dip by flexing the knees, allowing them to travel forward and without moving the hips to the rear. The dip should not be excessive. Return the weight to the starting position by driving forcefully though the feet.

en: This movement strengthens the dip portion of the jerk. Begin with the bar racked in the jerk position, with the shoulders forward to create a shelf and the bar lightly contacting the throat. The feet should be directly under the hips, with the feet turned out as is comfortable.

Keeping the torso vertical, dip by flexing the knees, allowing them to travel forward and without moving the hips to the rear. The dip should not be excessive. Return the weight to the starting position by driving forcefully though the feet.
es: This movimiento strengthens the dip portion of the jerk. Empieza with the barra racked in the jerk posición, with the hombros forward to create a shelf and the barra lightly contacting the throat. The pies should be directly under the caderas, with the pies turned out as is comfortable.

Manténing the torso vertical, dip by flexing the rodillas, allowing them to travel forward and without moving the caderas to the rear. The dip should not be excessive. Return the peso to the posición inicial by driving forcefully though the pies.

name: Jerk Balance
instructions: This drill helps you learn to drive yourself low enough during the jerk and corrects those who move backward during the movement. Begin with the bar racked in the jerk position, with the shoulders forward, torso upright, and the feet split slightly apart.
Initiate the movement as you would a normal jerk, dipping at the knees while keeping your torso vertical, and driving back up forcefully, using momentum and not your arms to elevate the weight.
Keep the rear foot in place, using it to drive your body forward into a full split as you jerk the weight. Recover by standing up with the weight overhead.

Keep the rear foot in place, using it to drive your body forward into a full split as you jerk the weight. Recover by standing up with the weight overhead.
es: This drill helps you learn to drive yourself low enough during the jerk and corrects those who move espaldaward during the movimiento. Empieza with the barra racked in the jerk posición, with the hombros forward, torso upright, and the pies split slightly apart.

Initiate the movimiento as you would a normal jerk, dipping at the rodillas while keeping your torso vertical, and driving espalda up forcefully, using momentum and not your brazos to elevate the peso.
Mantén the rear pie in place, using it to drive your body forward into a full split as you jerk the peso. Recover by standing up with the peso overcabeza.

name: Jefferson Squat
instructions: Place a barbell on the floor.
Stand in the middle of the bar length wise.
Bend down by bending at the knees and keeping your back straight and grasp the front of the bar with your right hand.  Your palm should be in (neutral grip) facing the left side.

Grasp the rear of the bar with your left hand.  The palm of your hand should be in neutral grip alignment (palms facing the right side).  Tip: Ensure that your grip is even on the bar.  Your torso should be positioned right in the middle of the bar and the distance between your torso and your right hand (which should be at the front) should be the same as the distance between your torso and your left hand (which should be to your back).
Now stand straight up with the weight.  Tip: Your feet should be shoulder width apart and your toes slightly pointed out.

Squat down by bending at the knees and keeping your back straight until your upper thighs are parallel with the floor. Tip: Keep your back as vertical as possible with the floor and your head up. Also remember to not let your knees go past your toes.  Inhale during this portion of the movement.

Now drive yourself back up to the starting position by pushing with the feet . Tip: Keep the bar hanging at arm's length and your elbows locked with a slight bend. The arms only serve as hooks.  Avoid doing any lifting with them.  Do the lifting with your thighs; not your arms.

Variations: You can also perform the exercise using wrist wraps.  In addition, you can use many of the other squat variations.  Finally, you can also perform the exercise with a wide stance or a closer stance.

Your torso should be posicióned right in the middle of the barra and the distance between your torso and your right mano (which should be at the front) should be the same as the distance between your torso and your left mano (which should be to your espalda).
Now stand straight up with the peso.  Consejo: Your pies should be hombro width apart and your toes slightly pointed out.

Squat down by bending at the rodillas and keeping your espalda straight until your upper thighs are parallel with the suelo. Consejo: Mantén your espalda as vertical as possible with the suelo and your cabeza up. Also remember to not let your rodillas go past your toes.  Inspira during this portion of the movimiento.

Variaciones: You can also perform the exercise using wrist wraps.  In addition, you can use many of the other squat variations.  Finally, you can also perform the exercise with a wide stance or a closer stance.

name: Incline Bicep Curls
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and lie back on an incline bench.
The dumbbells should be at arm's length hanging at your sides and your palms should be facing out.  This will be your starting position.

Now as you exhale curl the weight outward and up while keeping your forearms in line with your side deltoids. Continue the curl until the dumbbells are at shoulder height and to the sides of your deltoids. Tip: The end of the movement should look similar to a double biceps pose.
After a second contraction at the top of the movement, start to inhale and slowly lower the weights back to the starting position using the same path used to bring them up.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: You can also perform this exercise with cables or exercise bands.

Variations: You can also perform this exercise with cables or exercise bands.
es: Hold a mancuerna in each mano and lie espalda on an incline banco.
The mancuernas should be at brazo's length hanging at your sides and your palms should be facing out.  Esta será la posición inicial.

Now as you espira curl the peso outward and up while keeping your forebrazos in line with your side deltoids. Continue the curl until the mancuernas are at hombro height and to the sides of your deltoids. Consejo: The end of the movimiento should look similar to a double bíceps pose.
After a second contraction at the top of the movimiento, start to inspira and slowly lower the pesos espalda to the posición inicial using the same path used to bring them up.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: You can also perform this exercise with poleas or exercise bandas.

name: Incline Bench Pull
instructions: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lie face down on an incline bench that is set to an incline that is approximately 30 degrees.
Let the arms hang to your sides fully extended as they point to the floor.
Turn the wrists until your hands have a pronated (palms down) grip.
Now flare the elbows out.  This will be your starting position.

As you breathe out, start to pull the dumbbells up as if you are doing a reverse bench press. You will do this by bending at the elbows and bringing the upper arms up as you let the forearms hang.  Continue this motion until the upper arms are at the same level as your back.  Tip: The elbows will come out to the side and your upper arms and torso should make the letter "T" at the top of the movement.  Hold the contraction at the top for a second.
Slowly go back down to the starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: You can perform this same movement but with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).  You can also use a barbell.

As you breathe out, start to pull the mancuernas up as if you are doing a reverse banco press. You will do this by bending at the codos and bringing the upper brazos up as you let the forebrazos hang.  Continue this motion until the upper brazos are at the same level as your espalda.  Consejo: The codos will come out to the side and your upper brazos and torso should make the letter "T" at the top of the movimiento.  Hold the contraction at the top for a second.
Slowly go espalda down to the posición inicial as you breathe in.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: You can perform this same movimiento but with a agarre neutro (palms facing each other).  You can also use a barrara.

name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lay on an incline bench (between 30-45%) with a barbell in the rack above you. Take a medium grip (a grip which creates a 90 degree angle at your elbows half way through the movement) and lift the bar off the rack so that it is directly above your chest. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar gently touches the top of your chest.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your pectoral muscles.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
Caution: You should always have a spotter when attempting this movement. If a spotter is not available you should be conservative with the weight you are using.
Variations: Smith Machine Incline Bench Press or Incline Dumbbell Chest Press.

name: Incline Narrow Grip EZ Bar Skullcrushers
instructions: Grab a barbell (overhand grip) and lay on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) with the bar pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Keep your elbows as close together as you can to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the barbell towards the top of your bench/head. Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your head pause for a brief moment.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extenions.

Inspira as you descend the barrara hacia the top of your banco/cabeza. Be sure to keep your upper brazos stationary and your codos tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your cabeza pause for a brief moment.
Then mientras exhaling, use your tríceps to return the barra to the posición inicial. Your brazos should be fully extended once again and pointing hacia the techo.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Tricep Kickespaldas or Tricep Rope Extenions.

aliases: Incline Narrow Grip EZ Bar Skullcrushers

name: Horizontal Barbell Pull Ups
instructions: Set a barbell in a rack at waist height or if you'd prefer, set the smith machine bar at the same height.

Lay yourself down underneath the bar and adopt an underhand, wider than shoulder width grip. Your heels should be the only body part in contact with the floor at this point as your body should be in a suspended, straight, rigid position above the ground. Your arms should be perpendicular to the floor as your torso hangs from them. This will be your starting position.
Begin by flexing the elbow, pulling your chest towards the bar through the muscles in your back. Retract your shoulder blades and exhale as you perform the movement.
Once your chest is an inch away from the bar, pause for a brief moment.
As you inhale, return yourself along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Underhand Lat Pulldowns or Seated Cable Row

name: Hex Bar Deadlift From Frame
instructions: Load up a trap bar (also known as a hex bar) and stand inside it with a shoulder width stance.

Whilst maintaining good posture (a straight back), bend at your knees and hips (until your hips are just above knee height) which will lower your torso enabling you to take hold of the handles. This will be the starting position of the exercise.

Initiate the movement by pressing through your heels and quads whilst simultaneously use the muscles in your back to bring your torso into a vertical position. Maintain strict form (straight back) and exhale. Once you are in a standing position you should push your chest out by pulling your shoulder blades together.
After a brief pause, descend the frame back along the same path and inhale until you are back in the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be very careful when performing this exercise and if you suffer from any back pain, this may not be suitable movement for you.
Variation: Rack Pulls or Barbell Deadlifts.

name.es: Hex Bar peso muerto From Frame

en: Load up a trap bar (also known as a hex bar) and stand inside it with a shoulder width stance.

Initiate the movement by pressing through your heels and quads whilst simultaneously use the muscles in your back to bring your torso into a vertical position. After a brief pause, descend the frame back along the same path and inhale until you are back in the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be very careful when performing this exercise and if you suffer from any back pain, this may not be suitable movement for you.
Variation: Rack Pulls or Barbell Deadlifts.
es: Load up a trap barra (also known as a hex barra) and stand inside it with a hombro width stance.

Whilst maintaining good posture (a straight espalda), bend at your rodillas and caderas (until your caderas are just above rodilla height) which will lower your torso enabling you to take hold of the asas. This will be the posición inicial of the exercise.

Initiate the movimiento by pressing through your talones and cuádriceps mientras simultaneously use the muscles in your espalda to bring your torso into a vertical posición. Mantén strict form (straight espalda) and espira. Once you are in a standing posición you should push your pecho out by pulling your hombro blades together.
Tras una breve pausa, descend the frame espalda along the same path and inspira until you are espalda in the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Precaución: Be very careful when performing this exercise and if you suffer from any espalda pain, this may not be suitable movimiento for you.
Variación: Rack Pulls or barra Deadlifts.

name: Heaving Snatch Balance
instructions: This drill helps you learn the snatch. Begin by holding a light weight across the back of the shoulders. Your feet should be slightly wider than hip width apart with the feet turned out, the same position that you would perform a squat with.
Begin by dipping with the knees slightly, and popping back up to briefly unload the bar. Drive yourself underneath the bar, elevating it overhead as you descend into a full squat.
Return to a standing position.

en: This drill helps you learn the snatch. Begin by holding a light weight across the back of the shoulders. Your feet should be slightly wider than hip width apart with the feet turned out, the same position that you would perform a squat with.

es: This drill helps you learn the snatch. Empieza by holding a light peso across the espalda of the hombros. Your pies should be slightly wider than cadera width apart with the pies turned out, the same posición that you would perform a squat with.
Empieza by dipping with the rodillas slightly, and popping espalda up to briefly unload the barra. Drive yourself underneath the barra, elevating it overcabeza as you descend into a full squat.
Return to a standing posición.

name: Hang Snatch - Below Knees
instructions: Begin with a wide grip on the bar, with an overhand or hook grip. The feet should be directly below the hips with the feet turned out. Your knees should be slightly bent, and the torso inclined forward. The spine should be fully extended and the head facing forward. The bar should be just below the knees. This will be your starting position.
Aggressively extend through the legs and hips. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, forcefully pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. Receive the bar with your body as low as possible and the arms fully extended overhead.
Return to a standing position with the weight overhead, and then return the weight to the floor under control.

en: Begin with a wide grip on the bar, with an overhand or hook grip. The feet should be directly below the hips with the feet turned out. Your knees should be slightly bent, and the torso inclined forward. The spine should be fully extended and the head facing forward. The bar should be just below the knees. This will be your starting position.
Aggressively extend through the legs and hips. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, forcefully pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. Receive the bar with your body as low as possible and the arms fully extended overhead.
Return to a standing position with the weight overhead, and then return the weight to the floor under control.
es: Empieza with a agarre amplio on the barra, with an overmano or hook grip. The pies should be directly below the caderas with the pies turned out. Your rodillas should be slightly bent, and the torso inclined forward. The spine should be fully extended and the cabeza facing forward. The barra should be just below the rodillas. Esta será la posición inicial.
Aggressively extend through the legs and caderas. At peak extension, shrug the hombros and allow the codos to flex to the side.

As you move your pies into the receiving posición, forcefully pull yourself below the barra as you elevate the barra overcabeza. Receive the barra with your body as low as possible and the brazos fully extended overcabeza.
Return to a standing posición with the peso overcabeza, and then return the peso to the suelo under control.

name: Hang Power Snatch
instructions: Begin with a loaded barbell on the floor. The bar should be close to or touching the shins, and a wide grip should be taken on the bar. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar. This will be the starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the front of the heels, raising the bar from the ground. The back angle should stay the same until the bar passes the knees.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, a slightly wider position, pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. The bar should be received in a partial squat. Continue raising the bar to the overhead position, receiving the bar locked out overhead.
Return to a standing position with the weight over head.

en: Begin with a loaded barbell on the floor. The bar should be close to or touching the shins, and a wide grip should be taken on the bar. The feet should be directly below the hips, with the feet turned out as needed. Lower the hips, with the chest up and the head looking forward. The shoulders should be just in front of the bar. This will be the starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the front of the heels, raising the bar from the ground. The back angle should stay the same until the bar passes the knees.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the hips knees and ankles, driving the bar up as quickly as possible. The bar should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the shoulders and allow the elbows to flex to the side.

As you move your feet into the receiving position, a slightly wider position, pull yourself below the bar as you elevate the bar overhead. The bar should be received in a partial squat. Continue raising the bar to the overhead position, receiving the bar locked out overhead.
Return to a standing position with the weight over head.
es: Empieza with a loaded barrara on the suelo. The barra should be close to or touching the shins, and a agarre amplio should be taken on the barra. The pies should be directly below the caderas, with the pies turned out as needed. Lower the caderas, with the pecho up and the cabeza looking forward. The hombros should be just in front of the barra. This will be the posición inicial.
Empieza the first pull by driving through the front of the talones, raising the barra from the ground. The espalda angle should stay the same until the barra passes the rodillas.

Transition into the second pull by extending through the caderas rodillas and ankles, driving the barra up as quickly as possible. The barra should be close to the body. At peak extension, shrug the hombros and allow the codos to flex to the side.

As you move your pies into the receiving posición, a slightly wider posición, pull yourself below the barra as you elevate the barra overcabeza. The barra should be received in a partial squat. Continue raising the barra to the overcabeza posición, receiving the barra locked out overcabeza.
Return to a standing posición with the peso over cabeza.

name: Hang Clean - Below the Knees
instructions: Begin with a shoulder width, double overhand or hook grip, with the bar hanging just below the knees. Your back should be straight and inclined slightly forward.

Begin by aggressively extending through the hips, knees and ankles, driving the weight upward. As you do so, shrug your shoulders towards your ears.  As full extension is achieved, transition into the third pull by aggressively shrugging and flexing the arms with the elbows up and out.

At peak extension, aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your elbows under the bar as you do so. Receive the bar in a front squat position, the depth of which is dependent upon the height of the bar at the end of the third pull. The bar should be racked onto the protracted shoulders, lightly touching the throat with the hands relaxed. Continue to descend to the bottom squat position, which will help in the recovery.
Immediately recover by driving through the heels, keeping the torso upright and elbows up. Continue until you have risen to a standing position.

Empieza by aggressively extending through the caderas, rodillas and ankles, driving the peso upward. As you do so, shrug your hombros hacia your ears.  As full extension is achieved, transition into the third pull by aggressively shrugging and flexing the brazos with the codos up and out.

At peak extension, aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your codos under the barra as you do so. Receive the barra in a front squat posición, the depth of which is dependent upon the height of the barra at the end of the third pull. The barra should be racked onto the protracted hombros, lightly touching the throat with the manos relaxed. Continue to descend to the bottom squat posición, which will help in the recovery.
Immediately recover by driving through the talones, keeping the torso upright and codos up. Continue until you have risen to a standing posición.

name: Hang Cleans
instructions: Begin with a shoulder width, double overhand or hook grip, with the bar hanging at the mid thigh position. Your back should be straight and inclined slightly forward.

Begin by aggressively extending through the hips, knees and ankles, driving the weight upward. As you do so, shrug your shoulders towards your ears.   As full extension is achieved, transition into the final pull by aggressively shrugging and flexing the arms with the elbows up and out. At peak extension, aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your elbows under the bar as you do so. Receive the bar in a front squat position, the depth of which is dependent upon the height of the bar at the end of the third pull. Immediately recover by driving through the heels, keeping the torso upright and elbows up. Continue until you have risen to a standing position.

name: Good Morning off Pins
instructions: Begin with a bar on a rack at about the same height as your stomach. Bend over underneath the bar and rack the bar across the rear of your shoulders as you would a power squat, not on top of your shoulders. At the proper height, you should be near parallel to the floor when bent over.  Keep your back tight, shoulder blades pinched together, and your knees slightly bent.  Keep your back arched and your cervical spine in proper alignment.
Begin the motion by extending through the hips with your glutes and hamstrings, and you are standing with the weight. Slowly lower the weight back to the pins returning to the starting position.

At the proper height, you should be near parallel to the suelo when bent over.  Mantén your espalda tight, hombro blades pinched together, and your rodillas slightly bent.  Mantén your espalda arched and your cervical spine in proper alignment.
Empieza the motion by extending through the caderas with your glúteos and isquios, and you are standing with the peso. Slowly lower the peso espalda to the pins returning to the posición inicial.

name: Barbell Good Mornings
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across your shoulders. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees when unracking the bar. Press through your legs to lift the bar up and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a shoulder width stance, maintain a straight back throughout and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to lean forward from your waist. Push your hips and glutes backward as you do so until your torso is just off being parallel to the floor. Your hamstrings should be under tension at this point.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the barbell to the starting position along the same path, thrusting your hips forward, squeezing your glutes as you do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed. (Remember to keep your back straight throughout the whole movement).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from any back injuries, this may not be the most suitable exercise for you.
Variations: Barbell Hip Thrusts or Stiff Leg Dumbbell Deadlifts.

Step underneath the barra and take the peso across your hombros. Mantén a straight espalda and a small bend at your rodillas when unracking the barra. Press through your legs to lift the barra up and take a small step espaldawards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a hombro width stance, maintain a straight espalda throughout and keep your cabeza up. Esta será la posición inicial.

Inspira and begin to lean forward from your waist. Push your caderas and glúteos espaldaward as you do so until your torso is just off being parallel to the suelo. Your isquios should be under tension at this point.

Tras una breve pausa, espira and return the barrara to the posición inicial along the same path, thrusting your caderas forward, squeezing your glúteos as you do so. Colócate de pie to attention with your legs fully extended, espalda straight and hombros relaxed. (Remember to keep your espalda straight throughout the whole movimiento).

name: Barbell Front Squats Clean Grip
instructions: To begin, first set the bar in a rack slightly below shoulder level.  Rest the bar on top of the deltoids, pushing into the clavicles,  and lightly touching the throat. Your hands should be in a clean grip, touching the bar only with your fingers to help keep it in position.

Lift the bar off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.  Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out.  Keep your head and elbows up at all times. This will be your starting position.

Bend at the knees, sitting down between your legs.  Continue down until your hamstrings are on your calves. Keep your knees aligned with your feet by consciously using your abductors to push your knees out as you squat.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor mainly with the heel or middle of your foot as you straighten the legs again and return to the starting position.

es: To begin, first set the barra in a rack slightly below hombro level.  Rest the barra on top of the deltoids, pushing into the clavicles,  and lightly touching the throat. Your manos should be in a clean grip, touching the barra only with your fingers to help keep it in posición.

Lift the barra off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.  Step away from the rack and posición your legs using a hombro width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out.  Mantén your cabeza and codos up at all times. Esta será la posición inicial.

Bend at the rodillas, sitting down between your legs.  Continue down until your isquios are on your gemelos. Mantén your rodillas aligned with your pies by consciously using your abductors to push your rodillas out as you squat.
Empieza to raise the barra as you espira by pushing the suelo mainly with the talón or middle of your pie as you straighten the legs again and return to the posición inicial.

name: Barbell Front Squats To A Bench Clean Grip
instructions: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes.  To begin, first set a flat bench behind you and set the bar on a rack that best matches your height.  Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, bring your arms up under the bar while keeping the elbows high and the upper arm slightly above parallel to the floor.  Rest the bar on top of the deltoids and cross your arms while grasping the bar for total control.
Lift the bar off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out.  Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.  This will be your starting position.  (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances described in the foot positioning section).

Begin to slowly lower the bar by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up.  Continue down until you touch the bench with your glutes.  Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.  Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front.  If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor mainly with the heel of your foot as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This is not an exercise to be taken lightly.  If you have back issues, substitute it with the dumbbell squat variation or a leg press instead.  If you have a healthy back, ensure perfect form and never slouch the back forward as this can cause back injury. Be cautious as well with the weight used; in case of doubt, use less weight rather than more.  The front squat is a very safe exercise but only if performed properly.  This version of the squat is better suited for advanced athletes.
Variations: As previously mentioned, there are various stances that can be used depending on what you want to emphasize.

name: Barbell Front Squats Cross Grip
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across the front of your shoulders. You can either relax your wrists and place then under the bar to secure its position or you can cross your arms over and lift your elbows, which ever is most comfortable for yourself. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees when unracking the bar. Press through your legs to lift the bar up and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

en: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across the front of your shoulders. You can either relax your wrists and place then under the bar to secure its position or you can cross your arms over and lift your elbows, which ever is most comfortable for yourself. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees when unracking the bar. Press through your legs to lift the bar up and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.
es: Coloca the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barrara can lay across your hombros comfortably. Next, set the safety barras at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the peso and you will be safe. Load up the barrara with the correct amount of peso on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the barra and take the peso across the front of your hombros. You can either relax your wrists and place then under the barra to secure its posición or you can cross your brazos over and lift your codos, which ever is most comfortable for yourself. Mantén a straight espalda and a small bend at your rodillas when unracking the barra. Press through your legs to lift the barra up and take a small step espaldawards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a hombro width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Mantén a straight espalda throughout the movimiento and keep your cabeza up. Esta será la posición inicial.

Inspira and begin to descend by bending at your rodillas and caderas. Your peso should be put predominantly through your talones as you sit espalda into the movimiento until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the suelo.

Tras una breve pausa, espira and return the barra to the posición inicial along the same path, push through your talones with the force being driven through your cuádriceps to do so. Colócate de pie to attention with your legs fully extended, espalda straight and hombros relaxed.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.

Precaución: This is a very complex movimiento and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your pesos. If you suffer from espalda issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variaciones: barra Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Zercher Squat
instructions: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes.  To begin, first set the bar on a rack that best matches your height.  The correct height should be anywhere above the waist but below the chest. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, lock your hands together and place the bar on top of your arms in between the forearm and upper arm.
Lift the bar up so that it is resting on top of your forearms.  If you are holding the bar properly, it should look as if you have your arms crossed but with a bar running across them.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out.  Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.  This will be your starting position.  (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the foot stances section).

Begin to lower the bar by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up.  Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor).  Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.  Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front.  If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the ball of your foot mainly as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your arms when lifting the barbell.  It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form.  Also, make sure that your back is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.

es: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes.  To begin, first set the barra on a rack that best matches your height.  The correct height should be anywhere above the waist but below the pecho. Once the correct height is chosen and the barra is loaded, lock your manos together and place the barra on top of your brazos in between the forebrazo and upper brazo.
Lift the barra up so that it is resting on top of your forebrazos.  If you are holding the barra properly, it should look as if you have your brazos crossed but with a barra running across them.

Step away from the rack and posición your legs using a hombro width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out.  Mantén your cabeza up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight espalda.  Esta será la posición inicial.  (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the pie stances section).

Empieza to lower the barra by bending the rodillas as you maintain a straight posture with the cabeza up.  Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the gemelos becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the suelo).  Inspira as you perform this portion of the movimiento.  Consejo: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the rodillas should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front.  If your rodillas are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the rodilla and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Empieza to raise the barra as you espira by pushing the suelo with the ball of your pie mainly as you straighten the legs again and go espalda to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.

Precaución: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your brazos when lifting the barrara.  It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form.  Also, make sure that your espalda is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.

name: Standing Narrow Grip EZ Bar Overhead Tricep Extensions
instructions: Grab a barbell with an overhand, shoulder width grip and press towards the ceiling until your elbows are locked out. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance with your back straight, barbell directly above your head. Tip: Keep your elbows as close together as you can throughout to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and descend the barbell on a semi-circular motion down towards the back of your head. Keep the upper arms still and control this movement through bending your elbows and stretching your triceps. Remember to keep your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a brief movement once you have created an acute angle at your elbows. Then pull through your triceps to take the barbell back along the same path to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Overhead Tricep Extension (Rope On Cables) or Barbell Skullcrushers (Incline).

name.en: Standing Narrow Grip EZ Bar Overhead Tricep Extensions
name.es: De pie agarre cerrado barra EZ por encima de la cabeza tríceps Extensions

en: Grab a barbell with an overhand, shoulder width grip and press towards the ceiling until your elbows are locked out. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance with your back straight, barbell directly above your head. Tip: Keep your elbows as close together as you can throughout to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and descend the barbell on a semi-circular motion down towards the back of your head. Keep the upper arms still and control this movement through bending your elbows and stretching your triceps. Remember to keep your elbows tucked in.

Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Overhead Tricep Extension (Rope On Cables) or Barbell Skullcrushers (Incline).
es: Coge a barrara with an overmano, hombro width grip and press hacia the techo until your codos are locked out. Adopt a standing, hombro width stance with your espalda straight, barrara directly above your cabeza. Consejo: Mantén your codos as close together as you can throughout to initiate maximum tricep activity. Esta será la posición inicial.

Inspira and descend the barrara on a semi-circular motion down hacia the espalda of your cabeza. Mantén the upper brazos still and control this movimiento through bending your codos and stretching your tríceps. Remember to keep your codos tucked in.
Pause for a brief movimiento once you have created an acute angle at your codos. Then pull through your tríceps to take the barrara espalda along the same path to your posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Overcabeza Tricep Extension (Rope On Poleas) or barra Skullcrushers (Incline).

aliases: Standing Narrow Grip EZ Bar Overhead Tricep Extensions

name: Frankenstein Squats
instructions: This drill teaches you the proper positioning of both the bar and your body during the clean and front squat.

Place the barbell on the front of the shoulders, releasing your grip and extending your arms out in front of you. The shoulders should be pushed forward to create a shelf, and the bar should be in contact with the throat. Ensure that you only move your shoulder blades forward; don't round the thoracic spine.

Squat by flexing the knees and hips, sitting in between your legs. Keep the torso upright, the arms up, and the shoulders forward, and the bar should stay in place. Go to the bottom of the squat, until your hamstrings contact your calves.
Return to the upright position by driving through the front of the heel and extending the knees and hips.

en: This drill teaches you the proper positioning of both the bar and your body during the clean and front squat.

Place the barbell on the front of the shoulders, releasing your grip and extending your arms out in front of you. The shoulders should be pushed forward to create a shelf, and the bar should be in contact with the throat. Ensure that you only move your shoulder blades forward; don't round the thoracic spine.

Squat by flexing the knees and hips, sitting in between your legs. Keep the torso upright, the arms up, and the shoulders forward, and the bar should stay in place. Go to the bottom of the squat, until your hamstrings contact your calves.
Return to the upright position by driving through the front of the heel and extending the knees and hips.
es: Este ejercicio te enseña la posición adecuada tanto de la barra como de tu cuerpo durante la cargada y la sentadilla frontal.

Coloca la barra en la parte delantera de los hombros, soltando el agarre y extendiendo los brazos frente a ti. Los hombros deben estar empujados hacia adelante para crear un soporte y la barra debe estar en contacto con la garganta. Asegúrate de mover solo los omóplatos hacia adelante; no redondees la columna torácica.

Ponte en cuclillas flexionando las rodillas y las caderas, sentándote entre las piernas. Mantén el torso erguido, los brazos arriba y los hombros hacia adelante, y la barra debe permanecer en su lugar. Baja al final de la sentadilla, hasta que los isquiosurales entren en contacto con los gemelos.
Vuelve a la posición erguida impulsando la parte delantera del talón y extendiendo las rodillas y las caderas.

name: Barbell Floor Press With Chains
instructions: Adjust the j-hooks so they are at the appropriate height to rack the bar. For this exercise, drape the chains directly over the end of the bar, trying to keep the ends away from the plates.
Begin lying on the floor with your head near the end of a power rack. Keeping your shoulder blades pulled together, pull the bar off of the hooks.

Lower the bar towards the bottom of your chest or upper stomach, squeezing the bar and attempting to pull it apart as you do so. Ensure that you tuck your elbows throughout the movement. Lower the bar until your upper arm contacts the ground and pause, preventing any slamming or bouncing of the weight.
Press the bar back up as fast as you can, keeping the bar, your wrists, and elbows in line as you do so.

name.es: Press de suelo con barra y cadenas

en: Adjust the j-hooks so they are at the appropriate height to rack the bar. For this exercise, drape the chains directly over the end of the bar, trying to keep the ends away from the plates.
Begin lying on the floor with your head near the end of a power rack. Keeping your shoulder blades pulled together, pull the bar off of the hooks.

Lower the bar towards the bottom of your chest or upper stomach, squeezing the bar and attempting to pull it apart as you do so. Ensure that you tuck your elbows throughout the movement. Lower the bar until your upper arm contacts the ground and pause, preventing any slamming or bouncing of the weight.
Press the bar back up as fast as you can, keeping the bar, your wrists, and elbows in line as you do so.
es: Ajusta los ganchos en J para que estén a la altura adecuada para colocar la barra. Para este ejercicio, coloque las cadenas directamente sobre el extremo de la barra, tratando de mantener los extremos alejados de los discos.
Empieza tumbándote en el suelo con la cabeza cerca del final de un rack de potencia. Manteniendo los omóplatos juntos, saca la barra de los ganchos.

Baja la barra hacia la parte inferior de tu pecho o la parte superior del abdomen, apretando la barra e intentando separarla mientras lo haces. Asegúrate de doblar los codos durante todo el movimiento. Baja la barra hasta que la parte superior de tu brazo entre en contacto con el suelo y haga una pausa, evitando cualquier golpe o rebote del peso.
Empuja la barra hacia arriba lo más rápido que puedas, manteniendo la barra, las muñecas y los codos alineados mientras lo haces.

name: Barbell Floor Press

Lower the bar until your upper arm contacts the ground and pause, preventing any slamming or bouncing of the weight.
Press the bar back up as fast as you can, keeping the bar, your wrists, and elbows in line as you do so.
